programme.desc: A Naval intelligence ensign is killed in an unusual car accident. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Special Agent Ned Dorneget of the NCIS cyber division joins the team to further their investigation into The Calling, the insidious terror network radicalising youngsters via the internet. When it emerges young suicide bomber Bradley was not, in fact, affiliated with The Calling, Gibbs and his colleagues realise they might be back to square-one. Determined to bring the organisation down, the agents pursue two new leads, an arms dealer who may be supplying The Calling with their signature German S-mines, and another boy given a deadly mission by the terrorists. Guest starring Matt Jones

Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.desc: The investigators discover probationary agent Ned Dorneget has been bugged by Harper Dearing, the man behind a spate of arson attacks on US Navy vessels. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team tries to track down wanted terrorist Harper Dearing before another US Navy ship is destroyed, and a desperate Gibbs enlists the aid of a prisoner to draw his target out of hiding. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The aftermath of Harper Dearing's bomb attack on the NCIS headquarters dominates proceedings. As rescue crews sift through the debris searching for survivors, Abby, Gibbs, McGee and Vance are accounted for, but Ziva and Tony are trapped in a lift. Dearing makes contact after his ex-wife is taken into custody, enabling his car to be traced to an abandoned lot - and the agents arrive just in time to see the vehicle blow up. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team investigate the death of an armoury employee when divers find her body in a car at the bottom of a lake. programme.desc: The series skips seven years into the future after Joe's plane crashes over the Pacific and he is presumed dead. Now a lawyer, Allison has a vision in which he is still alive during a case

programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: Paranormal drama inspired by the life of medium Allison DuBois. Patricia Arquette stars as a woman plagued by visions of the dead, who uses her unique gift to solve murder cases. In the first episode, her husband Joe gives descriptions of her dreams to law enforcement agencies across the country - and one of the images bears a resemblance to a homicide in Texas. However, she comes into conflict with a cynical ranger who is sceptical about her ability

programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: The DA asks Allison to use her powers to select a jury for the trial of a man accused of murder and rape - to ensure those chosen will endorse the use of the death penalty. However, as she studies the case, she becomes convinced the defendant is innocent

programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: Allison dreams about a husband and wife who die in suspicious circumstances and discovers that her vision may be useful to one of Devalos's colleagues. However, she finds he is not very receptive to her methods. Patricia Arquette stars

programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: Allison tells Devalos she heard a witness lying to a police sketch artist to implicate an innocent man. Meanwhile, Bridgette's new playmate suggests another family member may be able to see the dead. Paranormal drama, starring Patricia Arquette

Starring Emilia Fox, David Caves and Richard Lintern, with Claudie Blakley, Lorraine Burroughs and John Dagliesh

While trying to work one another out, the team treads carefully in the case of a murdered vicar who received racist hate mail in the lead up to his death. Two-part story, starring Emilia Fox, David Caves and Richard Lintern, with Lauren O'Neil, Pippa Haywood and Shaun Dingwall

programme.desc: The annual St Peter's Fair gets under way in Shrewsbury but a gruesome murder dampens the festivities. Antagonism between local merchants and visiting traders becomes increasingly ugly, and as civil unrest rages in the streets, Beringar is ordered to kill his friend Cadfael, whose investigations are causing King Stephen acute political embarrassment. Medieval whodunit, starring Derek Jacobi, Eoin McCarthy and Michael Culver

Mark Haig has an electrical meter to take on, while wood wizards Josh and Oli are faced with a dated table. Sarah herself turns her hands to a vintage rocking horse

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah embarks on a hunt for a rural home in the Derbyshire Peak District with a couple of soon-to-be retirees who have a budget of £500,000, and for whom a pond is a high priority. Away from the house hunt, Sonali gets battle ready when she meets a master armourer and his apprentice who are upholding the tradition of this metalwork craft

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook

Liz is in the Green Pepper kitchen spicing things up with chef Romy Gill and Maddie is in the Red Pepper kitchen bringing out maximum flavour with chef Anna Haugh. Presented by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Antique camera restorer Brenton West tackles a vintage pocket camera, believed to have taken the final photo of a loving family before they dispersed. Amanda Middleditch and Julie Tatchell get to grips with a stuffed pig that is crying out for attention, while ceramics expert Kirsten Ramsay painstakingly pieces back together a delicate stone plate from India

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Despite Sharon telling new-found brother Zack to leave, he buys her breakfast - but when her back is turned, he swipes her keys. Kat wants her fling with Phil kept quiet, although after overhearing him booking a romantic holiday, she is secretly overjoyed. Kush visits Ruby about Arthur but she knows he has only plucked up the courage to speak to her because Martin is out. Bailey admits she won the science competition to Mitch, who assures her they'll find the money to attend the camp

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A pair of university graduates hope that their organic superfood powders will supercharge the Dragons' pockets. A low-calorie craft lager business brews up trouble over its high valuation, a dental-minded duo demonstrate their plaque-detecting app, while there's a heartfelt pitch from an entrepreneur hoping to up-scale her range of afro and curly hair products. Whether Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani, Sara Davies or Touker Suleyman will be prompted to invest in all - or any - of the products being pitched remains to be seen. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The final four amateur cooks interpret a classic dish of their choice before the best three face a Chef's Table challenge at Michel Roux Jr's acclaimed restaurant Le Gavroche, As usual, John Torode and Gregg Wallace assess the participants' skills to a bid to assess that they can be worthy finalists, joined in their endeavours by Monica Galetti, Steve Groves and Michel's daughter Emily Roux

programme.title: Avengers Assemble (2012)
programme.desc: A mystical artefact is stolen from a top-secret military base by Thor's brother Loki, who plans to use it to bring about an alien invasion. Spymaster Nick Fury brings together a team of superheroes, including Iron Man, Thor, Captain America and the Hulk, to defend Earth from the impending attack. Action adventure uniting the Marvel comic-book characters, starring Robert Downey Jr, Chris Evans, Chris Hemsworth, Mark Ruffalo, Scarlett Johansson, Tom Hiddleston, Jeremy Renner and Samuel L Jackson

In the heart of Edinburgh, paramedic Mat races along the tramlines to treat a patient who has fainted

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents from Oswestry Showground in Shropshire, with experts Kate Bliss and Ben Cooper, before seeing the items auctioned by Charles Hanson. Cameras also capture a Chinese treasure discovered inside a carrier bag on its way to a charity shop go under the hammer

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms and then sells three things about to be dumped at a tip in Witley, Surrey, before giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of the items. Items include some discarded oak and an old armchair. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to the Isle of Arran to house-hunt with a novelist and her husband who are in search of writing inspiration from the Scottish wilderness for their £375,000 budget. Along the way, Jules discovers the positive benefits to Arran's marine habitats and the local fishing industry since Scotland's first 'No Take Zone' was introduced

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: As busy mothers, friends Lucy and Hannah want quick delicious dishes they can cook for all the family. They are in the right place today as chefs Jeremy Pang and Akis Petretzikis are both wizards in the kitchen and masters of fabulous food against the clock. Presented by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Silversmith Brenton West is tasked with reviving a worn-down singing trophy that celebrates the musical talent of a sadly departed mother. Wood expert Will Kirk works wonders on an ornate, wired bird cage gifted to a daughter by the biological father she only managed to track down as an adult. Plus, leather expert Suzie Fletcher wrestles a beaten-up boxing bag back into shape

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michelle Visage round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Swimming great Rebecca Adlington, former England cricketer Jonathan Trott, Great Britain sprinter Harry Aikines-Aryeetey and Olympic hockey gold medalist Kate Richardson-Walsh join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kat refuses to go public with Phil while Kush's trial is going on. Phil takes it upon himself to sort things - by giving Kush advice on how to handle prison. Isaac is frustrated at not being heard. The Taylors gather to mark Chantelle's birthday, but Gray is nowhere to be seen - until he turns up at the club blind drunk

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: After an arduous six-week journey, the search for the country's best amateur cook reaches its climax. The finalists have to push themselves to the limit for one last time before judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace crown one of the three talented cooks MasterChef Champion 2021. Tonight, they must produce the best three dishes of their lives, push their culinary boundaries and produce jaw-droppingly impressive food for the judges

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Return of the satirical current affairs quiz, with guest host David Tennant and regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton joined by comedian Jack Dee and journalist Helen Lewis to recap the events of the week

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Tom Cruise, Jennifer Connelly and Miles Teller join Graham to talk about the much-anticipated release of Top Gun: Maverick. Felicity Jones discusses her romantic drama The Last Letter from Your Lover, Wunmi Mosaku chats about Bafta-nominated His House, and John Bishop promotes his tour Right Here, Right Now. Plus, music from Years & Years - fronted by It's a Sin's Olly Alexander

programme.title: War of the Worlds (2005)
programme.desc: A divorced father's rare weekend with his two estranged children descends into horror when alien war machines rise from beneath Earth and begin to systematically destroy every human being in their path - leaving the man with the dangerous task of getting his kids to safety. Steven Spielberg's sci-fi thriller, based on HG Wells' novel, with Tom Cruise, Dakota Fanning, Justin Chatwin, Miranda Otto and Tim Robbins

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents the cookery show featuring guest chefs and celebrities as well as highlights from the BBC Archives and wine choices for the dishes prepared in the studio

programme.title: Recipes That Made Me
programme.desc: Nisha Katona travels to Greater Manchester to discover family recipes from Bangladesh. First, Nisha meets restaurant owner Shahin, who is trying to introduce more traditional Bangladeshi dishes to his customers. Later, Nisha meets Liza, who teaches her how to make an authentic Bangladeshi fish curry

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Liverpool v Aston Villa and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester United

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles is joined in York by experts Nick Hall and Jonathan Pratt. The reds and blue teams have the choice of two antique centres to buy three items to take to auction in Darlington. Eric also finds out about the city's long association with confectionery at its Castle Museum

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: St Helens v Leeds Rhinos (Kick-off 2.30pm). Mark Chapman presents all the action from the third-round tie at Totally Wicked Stadium. The Rhinos won this competition last season, edging out Salford Red Devils 17-16 in the final, but they face the toughest of tests this afternoon against the reigning Super League champions, who perhaps surprisingly, have not lifted this trophy since 2008

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special edition of the quiz show, in which Bonnie Langford, Arthur Smith, Annabel Giles, Tony Hawks, Diane Louise Jordan, Melinda Messenger, Les Dennis and Richard Herring try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which a father and daughter from Middlesbrough, two forensic scientists from Oxfordshire, and best friends from London must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise songs from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Celebrity Investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond, Amanda Holden and Danny Jones assist two contestants from Sheffield in picking out the best singers and winning up to £10,000

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jan reaches her breaking point when Ffion is attacked by thugs working for Ross - who warns her that the next attack will be worse if she does not deliver the drugs as ordered. During a visit to the prison to drop off the drugs, she is drawn into helping a prisoner who has been stabbed. While witnessing a broken marriage, Dylan realises it is time to move on, and David realises that Rosa is not coping with Ollie's condition

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: Focusing on Osian's case and keeping Rose away from her family consumes Faith's attention, and she loses sight of the effect the divorce is having on her own children. Alys joins a demonstration against the working practices at a local farm, and ends up in trouble with the police. Rose hatches a plan to frame Evan for Gael Reardon's murder, and Faith finally gives in to her feelings for Steve

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches - Manchester City v Leeds United at Etihad Stadium, Liverpool v Aston Villa at Anfield and Crystal Palace v Chelsea at Selhurst Park. The Citizens were looking to further strengthen their position at the top of the table, while the Reds were looking for revenge against the Villans after a stunning 7-2 defeat in the reverse fixture, and Chelsea were aiming to maintain their good form under Thomas Tuchel

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker and is joined by Micah Richards and Alan Shearer to count down the top 10 Premier League transfer bargains

programme.title: Z for Zachariah (2015)
programme.desc: A woman lives alone on a farm that belonged to her family in the aftermath of a nuclear apocalypse, believing herself to be the last survivor of the human race. Her simple life is disrupted by the appearance of a second survivor, and they adapt to working together - but the arrival of a third individual threatens everything they have built. Sci-fi drama based on Robert C O'Brien's novel, starring Margot Robbie, Chiwetel Ejiofor and Chris Pine

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: The burglars targeting high-value properties, including the home of a Premier League footballer - and how they were caught thanks to a helicopter with heat-seeking cameras

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: Chris Packham shines a light on some of the species that show how brainpower, ingenuity and clever tactics are just as important as brawn in the daily battle for survival. Spotted hyenas play politics to gain power, and the female with the most friends rules their group, while being a team player can help when it comes to getting a meal - as demonstrated by vampire bats, who share mouthfuls of blood with strangers in the hope of building a bond and receiving the favour in return

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Competing in Cardiff for a place in the final are a renovated, three-storey semi-detached, a period semi with a modern twist and a rustic, shabby-chic home with a love of upcycling

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: With the wedding back on, it's time for Woody's 'groomspiel', and Big Mac has wild plans for it. However, Woody has made Tracy a promise he'll behave - which is going to be hard to keep. Meanwhile, new arrival Poppy and Arlo are getting on so well that Lindsay is jealous, even if Arlo is too innocent to realise

programme.title: Nature's Great Migrations
programme.desc: Documentary following 100,000 caribou as they face bears, wolves, frozen rivers and rugged mountains to undertake the world's longest land migration. Liz Bonnin and a team of scientists track the herd as they travel 5,000km through the Arctic wilderness of Canada and Alaska, revealing insights into the lives of individual animals as they attempt the epic journey. Cameras capture the caribou as they fight to reach their calving grounds before they give birth - failure would spell disaster

programme.title: Nature's Microworlds
programme.desc: Steve Backshall explores Monterey Bay, which lies just along the coast of California. He reveals how the unique geography and delicate balance of marine life, from microscopic plankton to visiting ocean giants, provide the key to its thriving ecosystem

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A collection of finds from various locations around the country, including Lulworth Castle in Dorset, Chiddingstone Castle in Kent and Wrest Park, Bedfordshire

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Radzi Chinyanganya, Hal Cruttenden, Kerry Godliman and Ebony Rainford-Brent test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: Transformations include a special recumbent tricycle that once carried an Olympic torch, and a widow's wish for her wedding and engagement rings comes true. Sabrina Grant hosts

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Architects Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison come up with virtual reality designs to transform a Halifax couple's 19th-century home. When owner Paula developed MS, it became vital to make the house more wheelchair accessible - but her partner Jeremy also sees an opportunity to give it a stylish new look. Laura's concept makes the most of the views of the garden and uses clever recycling tricks to stretch the budget, while Robert deploys off-the-shelf materials to making the place more accessible

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three chefs are halfway through their heat and the pressure is mounting. Once again, they each serve a unique take on the brief celebrating British invention and innovation. Whose inventions in the kitchen will veteran judge Paul Ainsworth consider the best? Only two will proceed to Friday's judging and have a chance to represent the London and the South East region at the regional finals. Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: Pandemic 2020
programme.desc: Told through candid interviews and personal archive, cameras reveal what happens as lockdowns continue around the planet and the pressure of being trapped indoors begins to be felt. Contributors include a French photojournalist who is forced to decide between working and home-schooling, and heads to the frontline of the pandemic

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of fellow comedians Tom Allen, Angela Barnes, Rhys James, Nigel Ng and Athena Kugblenu. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland
programme.desc: Paul Murton travels to the icy summit of Britain's highest and most wintery mountain, Ben Nevis, to discover how Scotland was first promoted as a winter holiday destination

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories, with Emma Barnett

programme.title: A Life in Ten Pictures
programme.desc: Documentary taking a look at a celebrity's life through 10 defining photos, with the star's secrets revealed by those who were there, and those who knew them best. The first edition focuses on Queen frontman Freddie Mercury, beginning with the earliest photo of the singer as a baby, and ending with a private moment captured shortly before his death

programme.title: Golf: The Masters
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the first day of the Major, taking place at Augusta National in Augusta, Georgia, where Dustin Johnson prevailed last year. With commentary from Andrew Cotter, Ken Brown, Iona Stephen, Mark James and Iain Carter

programme.title: Football's Darkest Secret
programme.desc: Three-part documentary exploring historic child abuse that took place in youth football in England from the 1970s to the 1990s and the code of silence surrounding it. The first episode focuses on former footballer Andy Woodward's 2016 revelation of abuse at the hands of a youth coach, and the personal accounts of players from various youth sides

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The last quarter-final sees the week's quartet of talented heat-winners return to fight for a semi-final place by facing the Critic's Test. The challenge - set by celebrated food critic Grace Dent - is to cook a dish inspired by global street food. Presented by John Torode and Gregg Wallace

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and the team travel to Hampshire to meet Kerie and Dave, to transform an abandoned shepherd's hut and pigsty into a unique B&B

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: Rav Wilding recounts the moment armed robbers burst into a jewellers' in Hinkley, Leicestershire. Even though they wore masks and gloves to hide their identities, they left behind a single footprint

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal maestro Dominic Chinea gets to work on a crumbling cast-iron garden gate, whose design includes the badge of the Royal Lancers, while horologist Steve fletcher and leather expert Suzie Fletcher join up to restore a wristwatch awarded for bravery in the First World War. Upholsterer Sonnaz Nooranvary takes on a rocking chair that has been at the heart of a family home for decades, and Geoff Harvey is given a 1920s penny arcade game

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which a sports lecturer, a customer adviser and a businessman must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three much-loved detached properties in Cardiff compete to be chosen for the final - a modern house with sea views, a country cottage, and a renovated farm house

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: It is the eve of Woody and Tracy's wedding, so not a great time for a power cut across the whole town, but everyone pulls together to make it a day to remember

programme.title: Golf: The Masters
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the first day of the Major, taking place at Augusta National in Augusta, Georgia, where Dustin Johnson prevailed last year. With commentary from Andrew Cotter, Ken Brown, Iona Stephen, Mark James and Iain Carter

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: James Lewis and Thomas Plant search for items in Birmingham bearing the city's hallmark, and Paul Martin finds out about Matthew Boulton, father of the Industrial Revolution

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Radzi Chinyanganya, Hal Cruttenden, Kerry Godliman and Ebony Rainford-Brent test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the week's overall champion set to be crowned

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: The team of re-makers rips up a silk wedding dress, and cuts up a 19th-century piano which has been in a woman's family for 140 years. Sabrina Grant hosts

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest scoring chefs from the London and the South East region cook their six-course menus again. They serve food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton, broadcaster Rachel Khoo, and guest judge, Professor Chris Jackson, a geologist and adventurer. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: A chance to catch up with some of the best bits of previous episodes as Paul Murton explores the nation's lochs, discovering the secrets of these iconic features of the Scottish landscape

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don adds self-sown ornamental grass seedlings to the paradise garden at Longmeadow, and Advolly Richmond explores the extraordinary history of lawns. Plus, Adam Frost explores the front gardens in a London street

programme.title: I'll Get This - Extra Helping
programme.desc: Extended version of the show in which five celebrities go out for dinner together. Jonathan Ross, Gabby Logan, Mark Wright, Anita Rani and Rachel Parris play a series of games, with the overall loser picking up the bill for the whole group

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: After a miraculous return, formerly missing pilot Starbuck claims she has been to the fabled planet Earth and can guide the fleet there. However, she is greeted with mistrust, and President Roslin believes she may be the fifth Cylon. Meanwhile, Lee Adama decides to quit the military

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: A rift grows in the Cylon ranks as the Six and Eight models battle with their human emotions and disagree with Cavil and Five over their attitude toward the raiders. Meanwhile, Starbuck tries to convince the Admiral that he is leading the crew the wrong way and Lee leaves the ship for a career in politics. Jamie Bamber and Edward James Olmos star

programme.title: Golf: The Masters
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the second day of the Major, taking place at Augusta National in Augusta, Georgia, as the players involved looked to make the cut. With commentary from Andrew Cotter, Ken Brown, Iona Stephen, Mark James and Iain Carter

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste report on a woman suffering from what she felt was a type of Stockholm syndrome, in that she was unable to escape a relationship with a man who took tens of thousands of pounds from her

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: The team builds a Harry Potter-themed den for Kiran, a nine-year-old who has recently had to change schools and move house to help look after his mother, who has mobility problems

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: A fan-club takeover of the programme

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: To flesh out the characters' backstories, Amelia insists viewers meet all their families - with harrowing consequences

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: After Poppy eats everyone's money, they must find a way to pay the rent

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to the rainforest in Ecuador in search of some of the deadliest creatures

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Roman Catholics Maria and Peter have their fifth child baptised in a traditional Latin service and in Stockton-on-Tees family and friends pay their last respects to June, one of the first women to be ordained as a priest by the Church of the England in 1994

programme.title: Me and My Dog: The Ultimate Contest
programme.desc: Dog owners and their pets compete in the final stage of the contest, taking place in the Lake District and hosted by Chris Packham. First, the Red and Yellow teams face a difficult canine intelligence test which involves Lake Windermere and some dog toys, with one pair proving just how far they have come. Then, the remaining competitors must take on a joint triathlon challenge, involving a two-kilometre bike ride, a 100 metre open water swim, and a 1.5 kilometre cross country run

programme.title: Golf: The Masters
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the second day of the Major, taking place at Augusta National in Augusta, Georgia, as the players involved looked to make the cut. With commentary from Andrew Cotter, Ken Brown, Iona Stephen, Mark James and Iain Carter

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four top chefs compete for the London and the South East region with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by British invention and innovation. One of the four contestants must leave the competition at the end of the episode, as decided by this week's veteran judge

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three chefs are halfway through their heat and the pressure is mounting. Once again, they each serve a unique take on the brief celebrating British invention and innovation. Whose inventions in the kitchen will veteran judge Paul Ainsworth consider the best? Only two will proceed to Friday's judging and have a chance to represent the London and the South East region at the regional finals. Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Highlights of the best finds by experts Anita Manning, Mark Stacey and others during the programme's travels around the country. Items featured include a set of Beatles autographs and a collection of Matchbox toys. Plus, presenter Paul Martin visits the birthplace of novelist and playwright DH Lawrence

programme.title: Live Formula E
programme.desc: The Rome ePrix. Live coverage of the second meeting of the campaign from Circuito Cittadino dell-Eur in Rome. Mitch Evans beat Andre Lotterer into second place here last season to claim both his and Jaguar Racing's maiden victory

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Catalans Dragons v Wakefield Trinity (Kick-off 5.00pm). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the third-round tie at Totally Wicked Stadium in St Helens. Catalans have won the last six meetings between the sides including a 36-24 win in last season's competition, and lifted this trophy for the first and only time in 2018, while Wakefield have not won the competition since 1963. With commentary by Matt Newsum

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of a family-run roadside recovery business in the Highlands, as they rescue stranded motorists on some of the most remote roads in Britain

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: The platoon defends headquarters in an exercise against members of the Highland Regiment, leaving Captain Mainwaring and his men with little choice but to spy on their rivals to try to come up with a plan to defeat them. Comedy first broadcast in 1969, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: BAFTA Film Awards Opening Night
programme.desc: Clara Amfo and Rhianna Dhillon host from the Royal Albert Hall as the British Academy celebrates the hair and make-up artists, casting directors, costume and production designers who have been instrumental in creating the best films of 2020

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1987
programme.desc: Some of 1987's biggest music stars look back on the year, which saw Margaret Thatcher secure a third term as prime minister, while the Channel Tunnel began construction and Britain was battered by the Great Storm. While those events unfolded in the outside world, the TOTP studio provided a haven for those who wanted to escape. Stock, Aitken and Waterman provided the floor-fillers as hip-hop and rap made their mark on the chart alongside the homegrown House genre. Contributors include Terence Trent D'Arby, Curiosity Killed the Cat, Rick Astley, Kim Appleby, Andy Bell, Carol Decker and Belinda Carlisle

programme.title: A Life in Ten Pictures
programme.desc: Tupac Shakur's image is known around the world as he was one of hip-hop's most photographed figures. But could just a handful of photos uncover new truths about someone we think we know? This edition throws a lens onto an extraordinary life by focusing on 10 defining pictures, from famous shots to private snaps, with their secrets revealed by those who were there, and those who knew the hip-hop star best

programme.title: Hip Hop at the BBC
programme.desc: Hip-hop performances through the decades from the BBC archives, including the Sugarhill Gang in 1979 and Run DMC, LL Cool J and Eric B & Rakim in the '80s. Plus, Ice T, Monie Love, Fugees and the Roots in the '90s and latterly Dr Dre featuring Eminem, Dizzee Rascal and Jay-Z

programme.title: Golf: The Masters
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the third day of the Major, taking place at Augusta National in Augusta, Georgia. With commentary from Andrew Cotter, Ken Brown, Iona Stephen, Mark James and Iain Carter

programme.title: Finding Jack Charlton
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke presents a profile of the World Cup-winning footballer, looking at his special relationship with Ireland after becoming the national team's manager, his complicated relationship with brother Bobby, and his final battle with dementia. including contributions from his wife Pat and son John, alongside celebrated Irish players, musicians, writers and politicians, including Larry Mullen, Roddy Doyle, Brendan O'Carroll, Paul McGrath, Niall Quinn, Mick McCarthy, David O'Leary and Andy Townsend

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild
programme.desc: The life of the wading bird, whose elaborate courtship dances have proved fascinating for those interested in wildlife. Julie-Christian Young tells the story behind these intriguing displays, revealing a turbulent and unexpected past

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming sets out on the last leg of his journey across central England. While crossing an epic aqueduct near Stratford-upon-Avon, the narrowboat enthusiast gets stuck in a lock in the heart of Birmingham

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Painter Bob Ross recreates a favourite escape - a rippling brook passing through the forest

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James and Helen are on their honeymoon and start the tuberculin testing, while Siegfried helps Tristan prepare for his final exams in Edinburgh

programme.title: The Elephant Man (1980)
programme.desc: In 1884, ambitious young surgeon Frederick Treves is intrigued by a sideshow freak billed as the Elephant Man. He finds, behind the disfigured exhibit, an intelligent person called John Merrick, whose rehabilitation he undertakes, with the severely deformed man's life being transformed by the friendship that develops between the two. Fact-based drama directed by David Lynch, starring John Hurt, Anthony Hopkins, Anne Bancroft, John Gielgud, Wendy Hiller and Freddie Jones

programme.title: Face to Face
programme.desc: Jeremy Isaacs conducts a one-to-one interview with the celebrated Welsh actor. First shown in 1995

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Treasures
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank travels from Turkey to Germany and takes to the skies in a hot-air balloon, before returning to terra firma, where he discovers a cavern with an unusual secret. In Istanbul, he climbs to the top of Hagia Sofia, revisits his past when his journey takes him through Poland, and resolves to find something of value from the wreckage of Nazi Germany

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Painter Bob Ross recreates a favourite escape - a rippling brook passing through the forest

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming sets out on the last leg of his journey across central England. While crossing an epic aqueduct near Stratford-upon-Avon, the narrowboat enthusiast gets stuck in a lock in the heart of Birmingham

programme.title: The $50million Art Swindle
programme.desc: Documentary telling the remarkable story of a charlatan art dealer who swindled more than $50million from the art establishment before going on the run. Michel Cohen, a popular and charming New York art dealer originally from France, was a high-school drop-out from a poor background who went on to become a rich and successful art dealer. Throughout the 1990s, he sold paintings by artists such as Picasso, Monet and Chagall to America's wealthiest elite. Cohen was living the high life until he began trading recklessly in the stock market and ran up considerable debts. In an attempt to recoup his losses, he swindled private collectors, auction houses and other art dealers out of more than $50million. When his crimes were discovered, he fled the US with his wife and two children and went on the run. In 2003, he was found by Interpol in Brazil and was imprisoned in Rio de Janeiro, but seven months later while awaiting extradition to the US, he escaped from prison and vanished off the face o

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first aired on November 29, with performances by artists including Dream Warriors, Dimples D, Chris Isaak, Pet Shop Boys, Soul II Soul featuring Kym Mazelle, Megabass, Patrick Macnee and Honor Blackman, Bombalurina featuring Timmy Mallet, Vanilla Ice and Deee-Lite

programme.desc: Mark Goodier hosts the show's 1,400th edition, first aired on December 6 1990. With performances by Twenty 4 Seven featuring Captain Hollywood, the Farm, Cliff Richard, Madonna, MC Hammer, Snap, New Kids on the Block, Vanilla Ice and Betty Boo

programme.title: Live Women's International Football
programme.desc: France v England (Kick-off 8.10pm). All the action from the friendly at Stade Michel-d'Ornano in Caen. The sides last met in 2018 when England recorded an emphatic 4-1 victory in the SheBelieves Cup. However, the Lionesses have struggled for consistency since finishing fourth in the 2019 World Cup, though new head coach Hege Riise's reign got off to a perfect start in February with a 6-0 home win over Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Old Grey Whistle Test Story
programme.desc: Jo Brand narrates a history of the popular music show which ran from 1971 to 1987. The documentary examines the music performed on the show, its rivals on other networks and exactly why presenter Bob Harris whispered. Featuring contributions by Jarvis Cocker, Annie Nightingale and Jools Holland

programme.title: The Old Grey Whistle Test: '70s Gold
programme.desc: Archive performances from the BBC music series, featuring Elton John, David Bowie, Captain Beefheart, Gladys Knight and the Pips, Curtis Mayfield, New York Dolls, Bob Marley and the Wailers, Brinsley Schwarz, Gil Scott-Heron, Blondie, Tom Petty and the Heartbreakers, the Jam, Talking Heads, Iggy Pop, Cher and Greg Allman, Argent, Steppenwolf, Lindisfarne, Dr Feelgood and Johnny Winter

programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Midge Ure and Kim Appleby explore the sounds that came from Scotland, Ireland and Wales. They start in Glasgow with the American influences that shaped a substantial part of Scottish music, look at the punk and folk backdrop to Irish music and, finally, delve into the Welsh merger of folk and punk. The programme features evocative archive, music and interviews with significant figures of the era including Bob Geldof, Clare Grogan from Altered Images, Pat Kane from Hue and Cry, Moya Brennan of Clannad and Mike Peters from legendary Welsh band the Alarm

programme.title: Synth Britannia at the BBC
programme.desc: A showcase of 1970s and 80s synth music taken from the BBC archives. Popular acts in the genre include Roxy Music, New Order, Ultravox, Soft Cell, OMD, Yazoo and Depeche Mode

programme.title: The Old Grey Whistle Test Story
programme.desc: Jo Brand narrates a history of the popular music show which ran from 1971 to 1987. The documentary examines the music performed on the show, its rivals on other networks and exactly why presenter Bob Harris whispered. Featuring contributions by Jarvis Cocker, Annie Nightingale and Jools Holland

programme.title: Patagonia: Earth's Secret Paradise
programme.desc: The documentary offering a glimpse into the rarely seen South American wilderness continues, this time following the path of the relentless wind sweeping east through Patagonia's dry desert from the Andes' peaks. The programme reveals a weird world of maras - a large relative of the guinea pig - and penguins, and witnesses the first faltering steps of baby guanacos - Patagonia's very own camels. Cameras also observe the people who have taken on this inhospitable environment and carved out a home for themselves. Santiago Cabrera narrates

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Paul Rose recounts the story of how American submarine USS Squalus became stranded in the Atlantic in 1939 after a fault caused a partial flooding of the vessel and the deaths of 26 men. The surviving 33 crew members were rescued by divers under the direction of inventor Charles Momsen, employing his pioneering underwater escape apparatus

programme.title: The Young Montalbano
programme.desc: Salvo and Livia start to prepare for their wedding and Mimi' Auguello is especially eager to be picked as Salvo's wedding witness. Meanwhile, a fire at a local hotel, which resulted in a man's death, is suspected to have been caused by arson, while the police receive an anonymous letter warning of the impending murder of a married woman at the hands of her jealous husband. In Italian

programme.title: Italy's Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Dr Michael Scott explore the Italian city of Venice, using 3D technology to discover how a city built in a swamp came to be one of the most powerful locations in Medieval Europe. The pair dive deep into the many canals in this unique place to experience how its buildings still remain standing to this day, and highlight how Venice's beauty once masked a ruthless secret state, and a world of excess and vice

programme.title: Masterworks
programme.desc: Michael Berkeley explores the history of Igor Stravinsky's The Rite of Spring, discovering that its 20th-century premiere ended in a riot. Conductor Leonard Slatkin leads the BBC Symphony Orchestra in a performance of the piece, complemented by choreographed routines by the Richard Alston Dance Company

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Zionne, Isaiah and Mandi are young black teenagers who have all experienced racism growing up in the UK. Mandi has been called racist names, Isaiah is often followed round shops by security guards, and Zionne feels she will always have to work harder than everyone else to succeed. In the first of a two-part special, they speak out about what life is really like growing up black in the UK and why things urgently need to change. After the death of George Floyd and the nationwide protests against racism, the teenagers know that this is their time to be heard. As well as opening up about their own experiences, they speak to their friends and their parents. They decide to create a manifesto to list the big things they feel need to change in society to make life better for black kids like them.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Formula One superstar Lewis Hamilton joins three black teenagers on a mission to make life better for black kids growing up in the UK. Zionne, Isaiah and Mandi have all experienced racism and have come together to create a manifesto to list the big changes they want to see in society. They enlist their celebrity heroes Lewis Hamilton, Nissy Tee and DJ Sideman to help get their message out there and discover that even the most successful black people have had to overcome racism along the way. Inspired by their role models, they are determined to make a change

Back at home, Chester and Elias play in the garden with their brother, going up and down the slide. Gia takes mum and granddad for a walk in the woods, where they see lots of beautiful things - they even find a bird! She starts by adding garlic puree, Korean chilli paste, soy sauce, honey, toasted sesame oil and her special ingredient, sesame seeds, into a jug and mixing them all up. Next, Hannah puts pieces of her salmon onto individual baking sheets and spoons the marinade on. She garnishes them with freshly chopped spring onions, and then her salmon is ready to be cooked in the oven. Hannah wears a colourful Korean outfit and treats her friends to some local Korean kimchi, but will her pals be able to guess her special ingredient?

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Febo's family are from America, where they celebrate Thanksgiving. As it is traditional on Thanksgiving to prepare and serve pumpkin pie as a dessert, Febo is making this for his friends. He starts by making a pastry case and fitting it into a pie dish. For the pumpkin filling: he pours melted butter and buttermilk into a bowl of brown sugar, along with whisked egg, cinnamon, ginger, nutmeg, allspice and cloves. Febo adds his special ingredient, pumpkin puree, and then mixes them all together. When it's mixed, he spoons it into the empty pastry case and puts it in the oven to cook. Febo's friends all help to decorate his American-themed table before they taste his pumpkin pie.

The story twists and turns as the Montague and Capulet families squabble, and Romeo and Juliet meet and fall in love. There are songs and dances to join in with as the greatest love story ever told comes to life in CBeebies' latest stage show performance.
