programme.desc: To celebrate 50 years since the first astronauts walked on the moon, the CBeebies Prommers head to the Royal Albert Hall to take a musical journey to the moon. Featuring music performed by the orchestra Chineke! conducted by Kwamé Ryan, with additional performances by the Chineke! Junior Orchestra and the first ever CBeebies Proms Choir, the pre-school audience can hear pieces by composers such as Handel, Puccini, John Adams, Benjamin Britten and Jessie Montgomery as well as the world premiere of Hans Zimmer's new work, Earth

programme.title: Justin's House
programme.desc: Justin is in a singing mood and has been singing so loudly all day that he has lost his voice. Ever the caring friend, Robert tries various funny ways to help

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. Mr Hammond has just got back from a canoeing trip in Alaska, and Andy is waiting for the mail to arrive. Over the radio, Jen informs Andy that the mail sack is in the Jungle Den. Mr Hammond found a bald eagle feather while in Alaska but had it sent back, so he is also waiting for the mail. Andy finds the mail sack and starts to go through it. Andy makes a terrible discovery as the seaweed he ordered for the aquatic plants collection has leaked all over the mail - including Mr Hammonds bald eagle feather. Everything is ruined! It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! Donning his adventure gear, Andy takes to the skies in his safari-mobile and jets off to Alaska. Andy almost flies into two bald eagles fighting in mid-air, causing the safari-mobile to crash-land in

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie, Doug, Jess and Gramps take Waffle to his first puppy training class

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: Topsy and Tim are growing out of their old, matching twin clothes so Mummy buys them new jeans, but Tim is not sure he is ready to wear bigger clothes yet

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Aleisha's family comes from Uganda. Today, she is cooking the traditional meal of rolex, which is a Ugandan omelette. Aleisha uses tasty fresh vegetables such as baby tomatoes, cabbage, red onion and her special ingredient, mild green chilli, for her omelettes. She pours whisked eggs into baking tins and puts her vegetables on top. Then her recipe is ready to be cooked for her friends to enjoy. To top off the occasion, Aleisha wears a wonderful Ugandan outfit and has a musical surprise planned for her pals. Will Aleisha's friends guess her secret ingredient?

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat
programme.desc: The veteran seafarer recounts the story of how the Rainbow got her stripes. Bernard Cribbins stars

programme.title: Down on the Farm
programme.desc: Storm Huntley visits a farm in west Wales to meet some spring lambs, as JB Gill catches up with a group of young gardeners to see what they grow and plant in their school garden. JB also sees how farmers prepare the fields ready for new seeds to be sown. Plus, Storm shares a spring-time poem and reveals how rhubarb is farmed

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Energetic animation for children aged two to five, following Duggee the dog and his Squirrel Club

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Energetic animation for children aged two to five, following Duggee the dog and his Squirrel Club

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters as they travel to the Valley of Balls in Kazakhstan. They soon find thousands of rocks that are almost perfectly round, but things don't stay perfect for long

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jet Cadets take to the skies in Argentina in an attempt to pass their Academy Gliding Test. As gliders do not have engines, Xuli is teaching two other pilot cadets how to glide using thermals to keep them up in the air

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Bob and Princess Purl are trapped on a runaway carriage and Bitz has to stop it, fast

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: B.O.T. is in Craggy Sandy World. The Hovering Honk is hunting for turnips. B.O.T. struggles to keep out of the beastie’s way as it sucks up turnips, chomps them and honks! The turnips get bigger and bigger and so does the Honk when it eats them!

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin goes ice skating with his friends and Mr Tumble builds a cosy den in his bedroom

programme.title: What's On Your Head?
programme.desc: Lucy the helicopter paramedic shows us her helmet. She tells us all about how important her helmet is, and how it helps her to do her job and help people.

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Mittens loses the new bell on her collar and Timmy helps her find it

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog and Pug escape a very noisy bookshop to find Granny Pug, who is leading a mediation retreat. They are trying to find a peaceful spot - sometimes it works and sometimes it doesn't. They return to the bookshop where Dog can make some noise

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: When JoJo can't find her favourite Captain Chloe storybook, Gran Gran suggests they make up their own story. As they create their pirate quest, JoJo learns that when you make up your own story, you get to decide what happens next.

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: Topsy discovers a lost cat sleeping on her bed, and the twins try to find out who it belongs to - but locating the owner is not as easy as they thought it would be

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: It's the hottest day of the year and Waffle is eating ice cream to cool down. Outside in the sweltering heat, Simon turns on the sprinkler to help everyone cool down, but poor Waffle doesn't like the sprinkler and darts through the hole in the fence to Mrs Hobbs's garden, looking for shade. The Brooklyn-Bells try to coax him back, but he refuses to return. Doug decides to make a mini swimming pool by filling the ball pit with cool water. Waffle loves swimming pools. Maybe that will encourage him back?

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. Mr Hammond is giving one of his special wildlife talks at Safari World. This one is all about a seabird called a sooty tern. Jen has deployed one of her remote-controlled bird-cams, which is painted to look like a sooty tern, to a small island in the Indian Ocean. The live feed from the bird-cam will be played during Mr Hammonds talk. Jen gets the feed up on the screen to show Andy. The footage shows a young sooty tern flying across the ocean on its fledgling flight. Suddenly, the image turns to static! Mr Hammond really needs that live feed for his talk and Andy realises there’s only one thing for it. It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! Andy runs to the Aqua Dome’s loading dock and boards his safari-sub, a hi-tech underwater submersible capable of travelli

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Motorbike rider Grace looks back at some of her favourite machines that are electric, fast and useful

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join Charlie, Etti and their families as they discover the world around them at three months old and learn what makes them both smile. We join Etti, who is having a fun day out with her family at some local gardens. They see lots of different flowers and animals including a big rabbit statue! But will these make Etti and her sister, Grace, smile? Meanwhile, Charlie and his family are out visiting a zoo where they see lots of different birds. Mum does an impression that makes Charlie smile, so his family use an instant camera to take some lovely family photos - but what will they do with them? After a quick stop at the local park, Etti and Grace decide to stop at a friend’s house to play peek-a-boo together. Grace creates her own happy faces with cut-out features. Finally we see what Charlie and his family were taking photos for - a photo album! What a fun and special way to look back on their family day out.

programme.title: The Toddler Club at Home
programme.desc: Ideas of mark-making activities to do with your toddler. See how mark-making activities with your toddler can help with their confidence as they learn to write. Hosted by Nigel from his own home. Join in with the Toddler Club Bop!

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Hugo learns about growing up as he continues to become taller, and wonders when it will end

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects associated with the colour purple, from lavender to purple sea urchins. We explore purple found in nature as well as the association of the colour to royalty. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour purple.

programme.title: CBeebies Prom: Off to the Moon
programme.desc: To celebrate 50 years since the first astronauts walked on the moon, the CBeebies Prommers head to the Royal Albert Hall to take a musical journey to the moon. Featuring music performed by the orchestra Chineke! conducted by Kwamé Ryan, with additional performances by the Chineke! Junior Orchestra and the first ever CBeebies Proms Choir, the pre-school audience can hear pieces by composers such as Handel, Puccini, John Adams, Benjamin Britten and Jessie Montgomery as well as the world premiere of Hans Zimmer's new work, Earth

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: After their snacks go missing, Sergeant Sarah and Constable Quack are on the trail of the biscuit burglar

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: Lola is a very fussy eater, she won't eat mushrooms, or peas or fishfingers or tomatoes. Charlie encourages her to change her diet

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: The animated adventures of an energetic baby monkey and his long-suffering mother, who take a light-hearted approach to problem-solving and teamwork as they tackle life's challenges together

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: A space dustcart drops its load on the Clangers' planet, including something surprising.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Tombliboos play hide-and-seek, while the Pontipines go for a walk around the garden and meet their neighbours the Wottingers

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Children's programme.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The renovation of a semi-detached in Bilston, West Midlands, and a mid-terrace in Chatham, Kent, as well as a derelict Welsh bungalow that turned into a four-year project

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: Rav Wilding recounts the moment armed robbers burst into a jewellers' in Hinkley, Leicestershire. Even though they wore masks and gloves to hide their identities, they left behind a single footprint

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: John and Suzanne race to the centre of Glasgow to help a homeless patient with a head injury. In Edinburgh, Moira and Dee struggle through the city centre traffic to assist a woman who has fallen out of her wheelchair, and use a clever piece of kit to help her off the ground

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross is in charge of two teams of bargain bunters at Nottingham racecourse. Expert advice comes from Thomas Plant and Nick Hall. Meanwhile, Charlie test the fairgoers' knowledge of hallmarks

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore looks round Earlswood Recycling Centre, trying to find rubbish she can rescue and revamp. Sarah enlists the help of her trusty transformers to take on some tricky items. Mark Haig has an electrical meter to take on, while wood wizards Josh and Oli are faced with a dated table. Sarah herself turns her hands to a vintage rocking horse

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah embarks on a hunt for a rural home in the Derbyshire Peak District with a couple of soon-to-be retirees who have a budget of £500,000, and for whom a pond is a high priority. Away from the house hunt, Sonali gets battle ready when she meets a master armourer and his apprentice who are upholding the tradition of this metalwork craft

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Sisters Liz and Maddie both love to cook and here wish to add some brand-new dishes to their repertoire. Liz is in the Green Pepper kitchen spicing things up with chef Romy Gill and Maddie is in the Red Pepper kitchen bringing out maximum flavour with chef Anna Haugh. Presented by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Antique camera restorer Brenton West tackles a vintage pocket camera, believed to have taken the final photo of a loving family before they dispersed. Amanda Middleditch and Julie Tatchell get to grips with a stuffed pig that is crying out for attention, while ceramics expert Kirsten Ramsay painstakingly pieces back together a delicate stone plate from India

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By Plaid Cymru

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan host the nightly magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Despite Sharon telling new-found brother Zack to leave, he buys her breakfast - but when her back is turned, he swipes her keys. Kat wants her fling with Phil kept quiet, although after overhearing him booking a romantic holiday, she is secretly overjoyed. Kush visits Ruby about Arthur but she knows he has only plucked up the courage to speak to her because Martin is out. Bailey admits she won the science competition to Mitch, who assures her they'll find the money to attend the camp

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A pair of university graduates hope that their organic superfood powders will supercharge the Dragons' pockets. A low-calorie craft lager business brews up trouble over its high valuation, a dental-minded duo demonstrate their plaque-detecting app, while there's a heartfelt pitch from an entrepreneur hoping to up-scale her range of afro and curly hair products. Whether Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani, Sara Davies or Touker Suleyman will be prompted to invest in all - or any - of the products being pitched remains to be seen. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The final four amateur cooks interpret a classic dish of their choice before the best three face a Chef's Table challenge at Michel Roux Jr's acclaimed restaurant Le Gavroche, As usual, John Torode and Gregg Wallace assess the participants' skills to a bid to assess that they can be worthy finalists, joined in their endeavours by Monica Galetti, Steve Groves and Michel's daughter Emily Roux

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Father
programme.desc: The emotional story of Jackie Saleh's fight to find her three children after their were kidnapped by their father in 1986 and taken to Yemen. Police discovered that Jackie's Yemeni husband Sadek Saleh had boarded a plane to Saudi Arabia, but the British government were powerless to trace the children. Three decades after their disappearance, her youngest daughter Sofia, who was a toddler when she vanished, is finally returning to Cardiff with her own family, fleeing the conflict in Yemen which saw their house being bombed. The programme follows the mother and daughter as they are reunited for the first time, as they attempt to form a relationship which was taken from them decades ago

programme.title: Avengers Assemble (2012)
programme.desc: A mystical artefact is stolen from a top-secret military base by Thor's brother Loki, who plans to use it to bring about an alien invasion. Spymaster Nick Fury brings together a team of superheroes, including Iron Man, Thor, Captain America and the Hulk, to defend Earth from the impending attack. Action adventure uniting the Marvel comic-book characters, starring Robert Downey Jr, Chris Evans, Chris Hemsworth, Mark Ruffalo, Scarlett Johansson, Tom Hiddleston, Jeremy Renner and Samuel L Jackson

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts revisits an old church in Denby, Derbyshire, which was being turned into a home in 2007, and there are also visits to Milton Regis in Kent and Liverpool

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: Rav Wilding reveals how a team of 'super recognisers' - police officers with an extraordinary ability to recognise faces - could be the key to catching criminals who commit multiple offences

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Medics are called to a patient who may be having a stroke, and help a palliative care patient decide whether to receive treatment in a hospice or a hospital. In Broxburn, Moira and Kelly help a palliative care patient decide whether to receive treatment in a hospice or a hospital. In the heart of Edinburgh, paramedic Mat races along the tramlines to treat a patient who has fainted

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents from Oswestry Showground in Shropshire, with experts Kate Bliss and Ben Cooper, before seeing the items auctioned by Charles Hanson. Cameras also capture a Chinese treasure discovered inside a carrier bag on its way to a charity shop go under the hammer

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms and then sells three things about to be dumped at a tip in Witley, Surrey, before giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of the items. Items include some discarded oak and an old armchair. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to the Isle of Arran to house-hunt with a novelist and her husband who are in search of writing inspiration from the Scottish wilderness for their £375,000 budget. Along the way, Jules discovers the positive benefits to Arran's marine habitats and the local fishing industry since Scotland's first 'No Take Zone' was introduced

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: As busy mothers, friends Lucy and Hannah want quick delicious dishes they can cook for all the family. They are in the right place today as chefs Jeremy Pang and Akis Petretzikis are both wizards in the kitchen and masters of fabulous food against the clock. Presented by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Silversmith Brenton West is tasked with reviving a worn-down singing trophy that celebrates the musical talent of a sadly departed mother. Wood expert Will Kirk works wonders on an ornate, wired bird cage gifted to a daughter by the biological father she only managed to track down as an adult. Plus, leather expert Suzie Fletcher wrestles a beaten-up boxing bag back into shape

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michelle Visage round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway explores the dramatic landscape of the north Gower saltmarshes, where he learns of the magical and mystical fairy folk, helps a sheep farmer round up his flock and even has a go at weaving. Plus, marine biologist Lizzie Daly tracks trout and Matt Bassett rides the waves on a new 'adaptive' surfboard at Caswell Bay

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kat refuses to go public with Phil while Kush's trial is going on. Phil takes it upon himself to sort things - by giving Kush advice on how to handle prison. Isaac is frustrated at not being heard. The Taylors gather to mark Chantelle's birthday, but Gray is nowhere to be seen - until he turns up at the club blind drunk

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: After an arduous six-week journey, the search for the country's best amateur cook reaches its climax. The finalists have to push themselves to the limit for one last time before judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace crown one of the three talented cooks MasterChef Champion 2021. Tonight, they must produce the best three dishes of their lives, push their culinary boundaries and produce jaw-droppingly impressive food for the judges

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Return of the satirical current affairs quiz, with guest host David Tennant and regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton joined by comedian Jack Dee and journalist Helen Lewis to recap the events of the week

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Tom Cruise, Jennifer Connelly and Miles Teller join Graham to talk about the much-anticipated release of Top Gun: Maverick. Felicity Jones discusses her romantic drama The Last Letter from Your Lover, Wunmi Mosaku chats about Bafta-nominated His House, and John Bishop promotes his tour Right Here, Right Now. Plus, music from Years & Years - fronted by It's a Sin's Olly Alexander

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce takes on the challenge of ditching her mod cons to embrace nature and help the environment. She tries giving up destructive habits like driving short distances and using plastic packaging to go camping in the Welsh Hills, where she meets a family whose members have embraced the delights of living off-grid

programme.title: War of the Worlds (2005)
programme.desc: A divorced father's rare weekend with his two estranged children descends into horror when alien war machines rise from beneath Earth and begin to systematically destroy every human being in their path - leaving the man with the dangerous task of getting his kids to safety. Steven Spielberg's sci-fi thriller, based on HG Wells' novel, with Tom Cruise, Dakota Fanning, Justin Chatwin, Miranda Otto and Tim Robbins

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents the cookery show featuring guest chefs and celebrities as well as highlights from the BBC Archives and wine choices for the dishes prepared in the studio

programme.title: Recipes That Made Me
programme.desc: Nisha Katona travels to Greater Manchester to discover family recipes from Bangladesh. First, Nisha meets restaurant owner Shahin, who is trying to introduce more traditional Bangladeshi dishes to his customers. Later, Nisha meets Liza, who teaches her how to make an authentic Bangladeshi fish curry

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Liverpool v Aston Villa and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester United

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter Q, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Swimming great Rebecca Adlington, former England cricketer Jonathan Trott, Great Britain sprinter Harry Aikines-Aryeetey and Olympic hockey gold medalist Kate Richardson-Walsh join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: St Helens v Leeds Rhinos (Kick-off 2.30pm). Mark Chapman presents all the action from the third-round tie at Totally Wicked Stadium. The Rhinos won this competition last season, edging out Salford Red Devils 17-16 in the final, but they face the toughest of tests this afternoon against the reigning Super League champions, who perhaps surprisingly, have not lifted this trophy since 2008

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special edition of the quiz show, in which Bonnie Langford, Arthur Smith, Annabel Giles, Tony Hawks, Diane Louise Jordan, Melinda Messenger, Les Dennis and Richard Herring try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which a father and daughter from Middlesbrough, two forensic scientists from Oxfordshire, and best friends from London must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise songs from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Celebrity Investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond, Amanda Holden and Danny Jones assist two contestants from Sheffield in picking out the best singers and winning up to £10,000

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jan reaches her breaking point when Ffion is attacked by thugs working for Ross - who warns her that the next attack will be worse if she does not deliver the drugs as ordered. During a visit to the prison to drop off the drugs, she is drawn into helping a prisoner who has been stabbed. While witnessing a broken marriage, Dylan realises it is time to move on, and David realises that Rosa is not coping with Ollie's condition

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: Focusing on Osian's case and keeping Rose away from her family consumes Faith's attention, and she loses sight of the effect the divorce is having on her own children. Alys joins a demonstration against the working practices at a local farm, and ends up in trouble with the police. Rose hatches a plan to frame Evan for Gael Reardon's murder, and Faith finally gives in to her feelings for Steve

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches - Manchester City v Leeds United at Etihad Stadium, Liverpool v Aston Villa at Anfield and Crystal Palace v Chelsea at Selhurst Park. The Citizens were looking to further strengthen their position at the top of the table, while the Reds were looking for revenge against the Villans after a stunning 7-2 defeat in the reverse fixture, and Chelsea were aiming to maintain their good form under Thomas Tuchel

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker and is joined by Micah Richards and Alan Shearer to count down the top 10 Premier League transfer bargains

programme.title: Z for Zachariah (2015)
programme.desc: A woman lives alone on a farm that belonged to her family in the aftermath of a nuclear apocalypse, believing herself to be the last survivor of the human race. Her simple life is disrupted by the appearance of a second survivor, and they adapt to working together - but the arrival of a third individual threatens everything they have built. Sci-fi drama based on Robert C O'Brien's novel, starring Margot Robbie, Chiwetel Ejiofor and Chris Pine

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Scottish Labour Party

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan host the nightly magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Despite Sharon telling new-found brother Zack to leave, he buys her breakfast - but when her back is turned, he swipes her keys. Kat wants her fling with Phil kept quiet, although after overhearing him booking a romantic holiday, she is secretly overjoyed. Kush visits Ruby about Arthur but she knows he has only plucked up the courage to speak to her because Martin is out. Bailey admits she won the science competition to Mitch, who assures her they'll find the money to attend the camp

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A pair of university graduates hope that their organic superfood powders will supercharge the Dragons' pockets. A low-calorie craft lager business brews up trouble over its high valuation, a dental-minded duo demonstrate their plaque-detecting app, while there's a heartfelt pitch from an entrepreneur hoping to up-scale her range of afro and curly hair products. Whether Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani, Sara Davies or Touker Suleyman will be prompted to invest in all - or any - of the products being pitched remains to be seen. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The final four amateur cooks interpret a classic dish of their choice before the best three face a Chef's Table challenge at Michel Roux Jr's acclaimed restaurant Le Gavroche, As usual, John Torode and Gregg Wallace assess the participants' skills to a bid to assess that they can be worthy finalists, joined in their endeavours by Monica Galetti, Steve Groves and Michel's daughter Emily Roux

programme.title: Avengers Assemble (2012)
programme.desc: A mystical artefact is stolen from a top-secret military base by Thor's brother Loki, who plans to use it to bring about an alien invasion. Spymaster Nick Fury brings together a team of superheroes, including Iron Man, Thor, Captain America and the Hulk, to defend Earth from the impending attack. Action adventure uniting the Marvel comic-book characters, starring Robert Downey Jr, Chris Evans, Chris Hemsworth, Mark Ruffalo, Scarlett Johansson, Tom Hiddleston, Jeremy Renner and Samuel L Jackson

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michelle Visage round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Swimming great Rebecca Adlington, former England cricketer Jonathan Trott, Great Britain sprinter Harry Aikines-Aryeetey and Olympic hockey gold medalist Kate Richardson-Walsh join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kat refuses to go public with Phil while Kush's trial is going on. Phil takes it upon himself to sort things - by giving Kush advice on how to handle prison. Isaac is frustrated at not being heard. The Taylors gather to mark Chantelle's birthday, but Gray is nowhere to be seen - until he turns up at the club blind drunk

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: After an arduous six-week journey, the search for the country's best amateur cook reaches its climax. The finalists have to push themselves to the limit for one last time before judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace crown one of the three talented cooks MasterChef Champion 2021. Tonight, they must produce the best three dishes of their lives, push their culinary boundaries and produce jaw-droppingly impressive food for the judges

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Return of the satirical current affairs quiz, with guest host David Tennant and regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton joined by comedian Jack Dee and journalist Helen Lewis to recap the events of the week

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Tom Cruise, Jennifer Connelly and Miles Teller join Graham to talk about the much-anticipated release of Top Gun: Maverick. Felicity Jones discusses her romantic drama The Last Letter from Your Lover, Wunmi Mosaku chats about Bafta-nominated His House, and John Bishop promotes his tour Right Here, Right Now. Plus, music from Years & Years - fronted by It's a Sin's Olly Alexander

programme.title: War of the Worlds (2005)
programme.desc: A divorced father's rare weekend with his two estranged children descends into horror when alien war machines rise from beneath Earth and begin to systematically destroy every human being in their path - leaving the man with the dangerous task of getting his kids to safety. Steven Spielberg's sci-fi thriller, based on HG Wells' novel, with Tom Cruise, Dakota Fanning, Justin Chatwin, Miranda Otto and Tim Robbins

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents the cookery show featuring guest chefs and celebrities as well as highlights from the BBC Archives and wine choices for the dishes prepared in the studio

programme.title: Recipes That Made Me
programme.desc: Nisha Katona travels to Greater Manchester to discover family recipes from Bangladesh. First, Nisha meets restaurant owner Shahin, who is trying to introduce more traditional Bangladeshi dishes to his customers. Later, Nisha meets Liza, who teaches her how to make an authentic Bangladeshi fish curry

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Liverpool v Aston Villa and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester United

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles is joined in York by experts Nick Hall and Jonathan Pratt. The reds and blue teams have the choice of two antique centres to buy three items to take to auction in Darlington. Eric also finds out about the city's long association with confectionery at its Castle Museum

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: St Helens v Leeds Rhinos (Kick-off 2.30pm). Mark Chapman presents all the action from the third-round tie at Totally Wicked Stadium. The Rhinos won this competition last season, edging out Salford Red Devils 17-16 in the final, but they face the toughest of tests this afternoon against the reigning Super League champions, who perhaps surprisingly, have not lifted this trophy since 2008

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie presents the classified results and a round-up of the afternoon's main football news. Plus, a look back at the week's talking points

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special edition of the quiz show, in which Bonnie Langford, Arthur Smith, Annabel Giles, Tony Hawks, Diane Louise Jordan, Melinda Messenger, Les Dennis and Richard Herring try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which a father and daughter from Middlesbrough, two forensic scientists from Oxfordshire, and best friends from London must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise songs from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Celebrity Investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond, Amanda Holden and Danny Jones assist two contestants from Sheffield in picking out the best singers and winning up to £10,000

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jan reaches her breaking point when Ffion is attacked by thugs working for Ross - who warns her that the next attack will be worse if she does not deliver the drugs as ordered. During a visit to the prison to drop off the drugs, she is drawn into helping a prisoner who has been stabbed. While witnessing a broken marriage, Dylan realises it is time to move on, and David realises that Rosa is not coping with Ollie's condition

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: Focusing on Osian's case and keeping Rose away from her family consumes Faith's attention, and she loses sight of the effect the divorce is having on her own children. Alys joins a demonstration against the working practices at a local farm, and ends up in trouble with the police. Rose hatches a plan to frame Evan for Gael Reardon's murder, and Faith finally gives in to her feelings for Steve

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches - Manchester City v Leeds United at Etihad Stadium, Liverpool v Aston Villa at Anfield and Crystal Palace v Chelsea at Selhurst Park. The Citizens were looking to further strengthen their position at the top of the table, while the Reds were looking for revenge against the Villans after a stunning 7-2 defeat in the reverse fixture, and Chelsea were aiming to maintain their good form under Thomas Tuchel

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Jonathan Sutherland presents highlights of the latest Scottish Premiership matches, which included Celtic v Livingston, Kilmarnock v Ross County, and St Johnstone v Aberdeen

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker and is joined by Micah Richards and Alan Shearer to count down the top 10 Premier League transfer bargains

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The renovation of a semi-detached in Bilston, West Midlands, and a mid-terrace in Chatham, Kent, as well as a derelict Welsh bungalow that turned into a four-year project

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: Rav Wilding recounts the moment armed robbers burst into a jewellers' in Hinkley, Leicestershire. Even though they wore masks and gloves to hide their identities, they left behind a single footprint

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: John and Suzanne race to the centre of Glasgow to help a homeless patient with a head injury. In Edinburgh, Moira and Dee struggle through the city centre traffic to assist a woman who has fallen out of her wheelchair, and use a clever piece of kit to help her off the ground

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross is in charge of two teams of bargain bunters at Nottingham racecourse. Expert advice comes from Thomas Plant and Nick Hall. Meanwhile, Charlie test the fairgoers' knowledge of hallmarks

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore looks round Earlswood Recycling Centre, trying to find rubbish she can rescue and revamp. Sarah enlists the help of her trusty transformers to take on some tricky items. Mark Haig has an electrical meter to take on, while wood wizards Josh and Oli are faced with a dated table. Sarah herself turns her hands to a vintage rocking horse

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah embarks on a hunt for a rural home in the Derbyshire Peak District with a couple of soon-to-be retirees who have a budget of £500,000, and for whom a pond is a high priority. Away from the house hunt, Sonali gets battle ready when she meets a master armourer and his apprentice who are upholding the tradition of this metalwork craft

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Sisters Liz and Maddie both love to cook and here wish to add some brand-new dishes to their repertoire. Liz is in the Green Pepper kitchen spicing things up with chef Romy Gill and Maddie is in the Red Pepper kitchen bringing out maximum flavour with chef Anna Haugh. Presented by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Antique camera restorer Brenton West tackles a vintage pocket camera, believed to have taken the final photo of a loving family before they dispersed. Amanda Middleditch and Julie Tatchell get to grips with a stuffed pig that is crying out for attention, while ceramics expert Kirsten Ramsay painstakingly pieces back together a delicate stone plate from India

programme.desc: Documentary about the disappearing world of the Belfast Jewish community, as it struggles to gather the 10 men, or Minyan, needed for a prayer service. programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker and is joined by Micah Richards and Alan Shearer to count down the top 10 Premier League transfer bargains

programme.title: Z for Zachariah (2015)
programme.desc: A woman lives alone on a farm that belonged to her family in the aftermath of a nuclear apocalypse, believing herself to be the last survivor of the human race. Her simple life is disrupted by the appearance of a second survivor, and they adapt to working together - but the arrival of a third individual threatens everything they have built. Sci-fi drama based on Robert C O'Brien's novel, starring Margot Robbie, Chiwetel Ejiofor and Chris Pine

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of the Science and Technology select committee session on UK science, research and technology capability and influence in global disease outbreaks with evidence from the Chief Medical Officer for England, Dr Chris Whitty, and the government's Chief Scientific Adviser, Sir Patrick Vallance

programme.title: Scottish First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of questions to First Minister Nicola Sturgeon in the Scottish Parliament

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Commons Science and Technology Committee session on establishing a new UK research funding agency, with evidence from the former adviser to the prime minister Dominic Cummings, and the business secretary Kwasi Kwarteng, from Wednesday 17 March.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Donald Trump's presidency is history. So not surprisingly historians are trying to find some perspective on a president who stress-tested American democracy like no other. Stephen Sackur speaks to Timothy Snyder, one of America's leading historians on totalitarianism and the Holocaust. He believes Trump and his movement brought America face to face with early-stage fascism, but is that pushing historical parallels too far? First shown on the BBC News Channel on 22 February.

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: The debate in the House of Lords on the 2021 Budget, from earlier today.

programme.title: Parliamentary Firsts
programme.desc: Selina Seth presents a short guide to the history of diversity in the House of Commons

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Three former chancellors, Norman Lamont, Alistair Darling and George Osborne, in conversation on 'tax in the real world' at the Institute for Government, from Tuesday 9 March

programme.title: Welsh First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage from the Welsh Assembly as First Minister Mark Drakeford tackles queries on issues affecting life in Wales

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his second Reith Lecture, Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, takes his audience back to the high drama of the financial crisis of 2008, which ended a period when bankers saw themselves as unassailable Masters of the Universe. More than a decade on, how much have the bankers changed their ways? How far has the financial sector changed? Dr Carney says that society must remain vigilant and resist the 'three lies of finance'. If not, it will live with a system that is ill-prepared for the next crisis. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Joint Lords and Commons committee session examining the UK government's review of the Human Rights Act 1998, with evidence from the former president of the UK supreme court Baroness Hale, from Wednesday 3 February

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Stephen Sackur speaks to American psychologist professor Laurie Santos, whose work on the science of happiness has won her an audience of millions far beyond the lecture theatre. With Covid putting enormous pressure on mental health, can we really learn how to be happy?

programme.title: Stormont: From Parliament to Power Sharing
programme.desc: BBC Parliament's Faye Kidd presents a brief history of the Northern Ireland legislature from Parliament to power-sharing

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Debate Coverage of the International Women's Day debate in the House of Commons, from Thursday 11 March.

programme.title: Ten Minute Rule Bill
programme.desc: SNP MP Douglas Chapman introduces his Bill to the House of Commons under the ten minute rule, covering the automated purchase and resale of gaming hardware to tackle the problem of online 'scalping' relating to games consoles, from Wednesday 24 March.

programme.title: The Treaty of Versailles
programme.desc: Professor Margaret MacMillan delivers a Gresham College lecture looking back at the Treaty of Versailles a hundred years later, from 4 June 2019

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: Conservative party historian Lord Lexden charts the career of Andrew Bonar Law from his birth in Canada to Downing Street, via Pembroke Lodge in Kensington

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Commons Home Affairs Committee session on the work of the UK government's Home Office, with evidence from the home secretary Priti Patel and officials, from Wednesday 24 February

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: HARDtalk's Stephen Sackur speaks to the Booker prize-winning author Douglas Stuart. His novel, Shuggie Bain, centres on a boy growing up amid poverty, addiction and intolerance in Glasgow. There are deep parallels with his own life. How does he extract so much love from hardship? First shown on the BBC News Channel on 18 February.

programme.title: Northern Ireland Assembly
programme.desc: Statement in the Assembly on updates to the Covid-19 response and social restrictions in Northern Ireland, with first minister Arlene Foster, from Tuesday 16 March

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Treasury select committee session taking evidence on the 2021 Budget from the Chancellor of the Exchequer, Rishi Sunak

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, observes that the pandemic has forced states to confront how people value health, wealth and opportunity. During the first few months of the crisis, most states chose to value human life more than the economic well-being of the nation-state. But if that seems to be changing how do we assess value in this sense? Dr Carney elucidates surprising differences in the financial value put on a human life in different nations - and goes on to argue that this reductionist approach fails to take into account deeper thinking about the worth of human existence. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: The idea of a social contract between the individual and the state is a staple of political philosophy. But what happens when that contract is threatened by forces beyond the control of any government, like a climate crisis or, right now, a global pandemic? Stephen Sackur speaks to Minouche Shafik, director of the London School of Economics and former top official at the World Bank. Is humanity capable of collective action to meet global challenges?

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of a session of the DCMS Committee on the economics of music streaming, with witnesses from Amazon, Apple and Spotify, as well as DCMS minister Caroline Dinenage, from Tuesday 23 February. First shown on BBC Parliament on 27 February.

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of a session of the DCMS Committee on the economics of music streaming, with witnesses from Amazon, Apple and Spotify, as well as DCMS minister Caroline Dinenage, from Tuesday 23 February. First shown on BBC Parliament on 27 February.

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of the Health and Social Care select committee session on the Health and Social Care White Paper with evidence from the Health Secretary, Matt Hancock, and Jason Yiannikkou, Director of NHS Legislation Programme at Department of Health and Social Care

programme.desc: Coverage of questions in the House of Commons to Prime Minister Boris Johnson

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Joint National Security Strategy Committee session on the UK's national security machinery, with evidence from former prime minister David Cameron and former national security advisor Peter Rickets, from Monday 1 March.

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Former Governor of the Bank of England Mark Carney charts how financial value has come to be held in higher esteem than human value, beginning by assessing what value actually means. How looks at whether the way it is assessed both shapes human values and constrains choices, and looks at how the markets affect society. Introduced by Anita Anand

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Despite losing the presidency and both Houses of Congress, Donald Trump still seems to have a chokehold on the Republican party. So what should Republican anti-Trumpers do now? Do they fight from within, or create a new Conservative Party? Stephen Sackur interviews Evan McMullin, one of their key organisers and strategists

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Former prime minister Tony Blair speaking at the Institute for Government on the last twelve months of the Covid-19 pandemic, its lasting impact and the challenges posed for politicians, from Friday 26 February

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: David Cornock tells the story of how Toynbee Hall and London's East End shaped the politics of Labour prime minister Clement Attlee

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of the Science and Technology select committee session on UK science, research and technology capability and influence in global disease outbreaks with evidence from the Chief Medical Officer for England, Dr Chris Whitty, and the government's Chief Scientific Adviser, Sir Patrick Vallance

programme.title: Scottish First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of questions to First Minister Nicola Sturgeon in the Scottish Parliament

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Commons Science and Technology Committee session on establishing a new UK research funding agency, with evidence from the former adviser to the prime minister Dominic Cummings, and the business secretary Kwasi Kwarteng, from Wednesday 17 March.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Donald Trump's presidency is history. So not surprisingly historians are trying to find some perspective on a president who stress-tested American democracy like no other. Stephen Sackur speaks to Timothy Snyder, one of America's leading historians on totalitarianism and the Holocaust. He believes Trump and his movement brought America face to face with early-stage fascism, but is that pushing historical parallels too far? First shown on the BBC News Channel on 22 February.

programme.desc: The debate in the House of Lords on the 2021 Budget, from earlier today.

programme.desc: Selina Seth presents a short guide to the history of diversity in the House of Commons

programme.desc: While on her travels, Mairi MacRitchie samples a crispy, light naan bread, the perfect partner to a curry. Mairi also makes her own bread recipes from her kitchen in Uist

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: A focus on ball sports, including bowling, tip kick and table tennis

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Eòrpa
programme.desc: Annabel Maclennan presents a look back at some of the stories that have featured in this series of Eòrpa, along with updates on recent developments. Among the stories were controversial proposals to regulate grouse shooting, a dispute over plans for a spaceport in Sutherland, and concerns at the lack of affordable housing in rural areas of the Western Isles

programme.title: Sgeulachd Edward Pritchard - The Human Crocodile
programme.desc: Documentary examining the story of Dr Edward Pritchard, who became the last person to be hanged in a public execution when he was convicted for two murders by poisoning in 1865. Presented by John Morrison, from the scenes of the doctor's crimes in Glasgow city centre

programme.title: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: Roddy cooks up Caribbean chicken with rice and peas and a Hungarian goulash with dumplings, while Uisdean rattles off a five-minute steak dish and a tasty pan-fried hake with mussels

programme.title: Eileanan na Banrighe/Islands of the Queen
programme.desc: Celebrating the people who live and work on some of the beautiful islands of the Commonwealth

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean hopes to swim through punishing currents to explore Gaylet Pot, an underground cavern near Arbroath

programme.title: Mach à Seo!
programme.desc: Ramsay MacMahon travels to the Isle of Mull to explore the longest cave in the Hebrides, and takes a trip to Duart Castle, where he meets the current clan chief

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Leugh le Linda
programme.desc: Linda Macleod travels to the Isle of Lewis to meet local nursery school pupils and read Cearban air an Allaban air Latha Fiadhaich! written and illustrated by Nick Sharratt and translated into Gaelic by Johan Smith.

programme.title: Creag nam Buthaidean (Puffin Rock)
programme.desc: Baba finds a very shiny shell, which unfortunately attracts the attention of the seagulls

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte
programme.desc: A birthday party has been organised for Crisdean, but will he get to leave the lighthouse?

programme.title: Nannag a' Noo/Huggleboo
programme.desc: Huggleboo is staying at Grandma and Grandpa Moustache’s farm and loves to play with the cat named Cat

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Marie MacAulay reads Maya agus Cat, written and illustrated by Caroline Magerl, and translated into Gaelic by Morag Stewart

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: The story of a grey seal preparing for life in the North Atlantic. On a cold, wet and windy beach, Sully must learn all he needs to know in just four short weeks before heading out to sea alone on the journey of a lifetime

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Everyone is excited about the start of the imminent New Year and Coinneach is trying to spoil everything, as usual - but he won't get away with his scandalous behaviour

programme.title: Tir is Teanga (Language and Landscape)

programme.title: Dealbhan Fraoich (Heather's Portraits)
programme.desc: Islay-born artist Heather Dewar travels to Bishopbriggs, where she meets former teacher and lecturer Rachel MacPherson, and draws a colour portrait of her

programme.title: Teicheadh bho Loch Nis/Escape from Loch Ness
programme.desc: After a rare Second World War 'Catalina' flying boat becomes stranded on Loch Ness, the race is on to repair the aircraft and return it safely to the air

programme.desc: Ross Couper and Tom Oakes perform one of their sets rooted in Scottish and Irish traditions

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: The murder of Sarah Everard has opened a wider debate about the safety of women on UK streets, with many sharing their stories for the first time. Could this be a moment for real change? Julie Etchingham reports

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lydia gets terrible news, she and Sam offer support, and Tracy tries to lay down the law to Faith

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family
programme.desc: In the first heat, Vanessa Feltz takes on Matthew Wright with both of their families in tow. They'll face a number of fiendish general knowledge-based rounds as they attempt to make it to the semi final. Who will make it through and get closer to the chance of winning £25,000 for their chosen charity? Anne Hegerty presents

programme.title: The Queen Unseen
programme.desc: The Queen's coronation made her the most famous woman in the world. But her anointment, the moment she became Queen Elizabeth II, was so sacred it was hidden and not allowed to be filmed. In this documentary, a clinical psychologist unpicks her guarded body language, and lip-readers breathe new life into iconic Coronation balcony archive

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: John Bishop's Great Whale Rescue
programme.desc: Part two of two. John's mission to return Little White and Little Grey from captivity to the Icelandic beluga whale sanctuary continues, as the pair begin the 6,000-mile journey to reach their new home. Following the previous delays in China, the release is already two months behind schedule, and as autumn approaches the whales have little time to put on enough weight and acclimatise to the cold water they will face in the bay

programme.title: Tenable

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Faith makes the best of an opportunity. Gabby is concerned for Kim. Charles is confused by forthcoming arrangements

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick fills Sarah and David in on why Leanne has to move away and so forces him to choose between her and Sam. Peter is stunned when the consultant reveals that he may not be eligible for a liver transplant. Steve asks Dev to stop stealing the Kabin's business

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The irascible copper finds his methods are called into question when a colleague is seriously injured during an attempt to thwart a suicide. The murder of a widow soon gives Frost a chance to prove his worth again, but the investigation only reveals an intriguing web of deceit, and it isn't until a forensic psychologist joins the team that they realise a serial killer is at work. With David Jason, Philip Jackson, Jeff Rawle and Danny Webb

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: It's Clarkson on TV
programme.desc: Some television is better than ever, while some of it is atrocious - and Jeremy Clarkson is going to say exactly what he makes of it. In this programme, he unpicks popular TV events and unearths wonderful hidden gems, with an eye for detail and a rogue sense of humour

programme.title: Alan Carr's Epic Gameshow
programme.desc: The comedian returns with the programme in which some of the nation's all-time favourite game shows are rebooted, beginning with a star-studded version of Play Your Cards Right. Couples including Harry and Sandra Redknapp, and Martin and Shirlie Kemp take part to be in with a chance of winning the jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Against Murtaugh's advice, Cole reaches out to Tom Barnes for help with a case that hits close to home, thus putting the duo's partnership put to the test. Meanwhile, Trish prepares to announce her campaign for district attorney. Damon Wayans, Seann William Scott and Keesha Sharp star

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Dino Dana
programme.desc: Dana and Grandma decide which one out of three dinosaurs would win in a fight, based on their size, strength, and various skills

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo is babysitting the Neighbours' toddler. The baby flees and kidnaps Fizz mistaking him for a plush toy.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz has invented a machine which stops toast from landing butter-side down. Magoo sees some floating toast and believes a ghost is haunting their house.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he is on a beach and that sailors are emptying their oil tanker right into the ocean.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: A raging forest fire will require the special skills of all five Tracy brothers to stop

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The hapless hero visits the Science Museum, where he makes a new friend with a bagful of gadgets

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: An old school friend turns up on Mr Bean's doorstep and is soon eating him out of house and home. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: The book that Mike is reading on Vampires is scarier than he bargained for

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: Don't Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: An immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Dave enters Fuzzypeg into a competition to become the face of a new brand of dog food which he unexpectedly wins, but as Fuzzypeg embarks on a glamorous new lifestyle, Dave misses his pal and seeks a way to terminate his contract.

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined by singer Marti Pellow, who also performs, and there are recipes for mussels, sole, and chocolate fondant, as well a masterclass in making omelettes. Chefs Gareth Ward and Dipna Anand also drop by with delicious creations of their own

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriot returns to prepare tasty recipes and chat with celebrity guests, who reminisce about their favourite meals. In the first programme he recreates a comforting Irish stew made by his friend's granny, and is joined by fellow chef Brian Turner and EastEnders star Jessica Plummer

programme.title: The Virtual Grand National
programme.desc: Nick Luck returns with the computer which forecast Tiger Roll's win in 2018 and asks it to take on the experts and reveal how the Randox Grand National may be won or lost. Richard Pitman and Alice Plunkett provide analysis, with commentary from Stewart Machin

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of the supporting races from Aintree, including the 3.40 Stayers' Hurdle. With analysis from Ruby Walsh, AP McCoy and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Alice Plunkett, Matt Chapman, Luke Harvey and Rishi Persad, Social Stable news with Oli Bell and Chris Hughes, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of the 5.15 Randox Grand National, which was won by Tiger Roll for the second year running when the race was last held in 2019. With analysis from AP McCoy, Ruby Walsh and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Alice Plunkett, Rishi Persad, Luke Harvey and Matt Chapman, social stable reaction from Chris Hughes, and commentary by Richard Hoiles, Mark Johnson and Ian Bartlett. Plus, Oli Bell hosts the re-run straight after the race

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a special edition of the classic game show, with Gemma Atkinson, Karen Hauer and Josh Widdicombe all trying to 'say what they see' as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen charities

programme.title: Game of Talents
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts a game show in which contestants pair up with celebrities to try to uncover the secret talents of the eight mystery performers in each episode. With big money prizes up for grabs if they get it right, will they be able to tell the fire-eater from the footballer or the line dancer from the lion tamer based solely on the performers' appearances and some intriguing clues?

programme.title: Alan Carr's Epic Gameshow
programme.desc: The comedian hosts a supersized reboot of Jim Bowen's much-loved darts quiz Bullseye, with four pairs of contestants taking on the questions and the dartboard for chance to win big prizes in the epic end game. Richard Ashdown keeps the scores.

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The chat show returns, with Strictly Come Dancing professional Oti Mabuse, This Country co-creator and star Daisy May Cooper, father and son duo Martin and Roman Kemp, and comedian Jimmy Carr joining Jonathan. Plus, music by singer-songwriter James Arthur

programme.title: Inception (2010)
programme.desc: A talented thief uses hi-tech devices to enter other people's dreams so he can steal their secrets. An industrialist hires him to perform a far more challenging job - to implant an idea into a corporate heir's mind, so he will think it is his own. However, the mission is compromised by the thief's own troubled psyche. Christopher Nolan's sci-fi thriller, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, Elliot Page, Joseph Gordon-Levitt and Tom Hardy

programme.title: The Virtual Grand National

programme.title: ITV Racing Live

programme.desc: Ever since a beautiful American woman arrived in the peaceful village of Wychwood-under-Ashe, people have begun to die in a series of apparent accidents. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, Benedict Cumberbatch, David Haig, Sylvia Syms, Margo Stilley and Hugo Speer

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.title: Wycliffe

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway appears to have got over his fear of Lewis treading over his newly promoted toes. Their opposites have attracted in the past, and there seems no reason to suspect that they won't now the younger man has got used to the idea of his former mentor coming out of retirement to lend him a hand. Even DS Maddox isn't getting on Hathaway's nerves quite as much, so this rejuvenated trio sets out to discover who murdered American classics student Rose Anderson, whose body has been hauled from the canal with neck and abdomen wounds. Nevertheless, a few red herrings threaten to get in their way of landing the killer. Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox and Angela Griffin star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks agrees to help old friend and partner Jimmy Valentine with an undercover drug sting, but when the bust goes wrong and a dealer ends up dead, suspicion falls on the officers. As Devlin pushes for the truth, the spotlight turns on Brooks' dark past, and a 10-year-old case involving no witnesses and a large quantity of missing heroin. Guest starring Hustle's Robert Glenister, former Rising Damp star Don Warrington and Anna Chancellor (Four Weddings and a Funeral), alongside Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Wycliffe

Detective drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Robert Hardy, Sheila Gish, John Gielgud and Rachel Weisz

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kelly plans to spend Roy's wages on furniture. Eric confronts Stella about her winnings. Zak and Butch argue about Mandy. Scott tries to put things right with Roy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Samantha decides to leave Weatherfield and move to London. Fiona struggles to keep control of her emotions. Roy tries to build up the courage to declare his love for Hayley

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona brings Morgan home to her flat. Gail is furious when Leanne gatecrashes her family meal. Curly asks Spider to help him write his speech about the environment

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The discovery of a body at a ramshackle farm alerts Wycliffe to the fact that a murderer is at large in a sleepy Cornish village - and when two locals disappear, he realises it will take all his deductive powers to solve the mystery. Whodunit, starring Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The doctor heads for London to meet the man leading the selection process for the job he wants, but has to conquer his blood phobia once and for all to secure the prestigious position. Back in Cornwall, Aunt Joan locks a mischievous schoolboy in a chicken coop - and faces the consequences when he falls ill. Drama, guest starring Nicholas Le Prevost and Gwen Taylor, with Martin Clunes and Stephanie Cole

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The new midwife in the village persuades Louisa to take a natural, holistic approach to treating an infection, infuriating Martin, who insists on conventional medicine. Meanwhile, Bert is smitten with the flirtatious new cook working in his restaurant, and the doctor hears good news about the job in London. Gemma Craven guest stars, with Martin Clunes, Caroline Catz and Ian McNeice

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Two children go on trial for killing a toddler, each blaming the other, and the case hinges on whether forensic evidence can prove who is guilty of strangling the boy to death. CPS director George Castle defies his superiors and takes instruction from the victim's mother, who has a surprising point of view on the crime. Drama, guest starring Deborah Findlay (Cranford), with Bill Paterson

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A magistrate acquaintance of Wycliffe is found hanged and all the evidence points to suicide, but the Cornish copper remains unconvinced and pursues an unorthodox investigation, eventually forcing the forensic pathologist to reconsider his verdict. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A man accused of murdering five people is killed in prison shortly before going to trial, wrapping up a lot of loose ends and saving the state the expense of keeping him behind bars indefinitely. Alas, Morse cannot help wondering why the body of one of his victims was never found - and the chance discovery of a corpse gives him the perfect opportunity to investigate further. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star
