programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus Tyrone accuses Fiz of shopping Alina to Immigration. One of Harvey's dealers, Ned, turns up at Leanne's flat and explains that he will be using the place to bag up drugs. Faye and Craig's plans for a romantic afternoon are ruined by Elaine

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Pilot episode of the detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd as the Cornish copper. When a bookshop owner is murdered in Penzance, the detective discovers all is not harmonious between the members of one of the area's most prominent families. With Kevin Quarmby and Carla Mendonca

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The mysterious shooting of archaeology student Celia Dawe and the significance of matching playing cards lead Wycliffe on a manhunt across Cornwall. Police drama based on the novel by WJ Burley, guest starring Bill Nighy alongside Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The apparent suicide of an amateur flautist and the disappearance of a maid on a secluded country estate soon have Detective Superintendent Charles Wycliffe unravelling a mystery involving family feuds and illicit relationships. Police drama, starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The detective discovers his half-sister's daughter has committed suicide and sets out to find what prompted it, entering the world of teenage rave parties and drug-taking. Meanwhile, Lewis has his sights set on promotion and studies for exams. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Sorcha Cusack, Jason Isaacs and John Junkin

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A former bookie falls under suspicion for the murder of a hospital benefactor, who is found dead in his car. As the detectives investigate the case, it becomes clear their prime suspect had a strong motive, believing the victim was responsible for putting his daughter into a coma. Brian Cox guest stars, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A planning dispute leads to murder after a stalwart in the Midsomer Conservation Society suspects her neighbours are involved in a money-making development scam. Political wrangling, burglaries and sexual liaisons abound in picturesque Great Pelfe as Barnaby and Jones investigate. Guest starring Hugo Speer (Bleak House), with John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Jane Wymark

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby reluctantly joins wife Joyce on a spa weekend at Swavely Manor, a country house offering treatments to soothe the mind and body. However, as he attempts to unwind, a woman is found dead in a flotation chamber and he promptly abandons his therapy to investigate - but the body count soon rises. The detective eventually wraps up the case, but it proves to be his last as he makes a big decision about his future. John Nettles' final appearance in the long-running series, with guest stars Ronni Ancona, Geraldine James and Lesley Manville

programme.title: The Suspicions of Mr Whicher
programme.desc: Feature-length period drama based on the book by Kate Summerscale. Inspector Jack Whicher is part of the newly formed Scotland Yard detective department and travels to Rode, Wiltshire, to investigate a murder at a country house. With no material evidence and only an inept local police force to help, he faces the unenviable task of solving a case in which members of the grieving family are the main suspects. Starring Paddy Considine, Peter Capaldi and Emma Fielding

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Unexplained killings rock the Bodmin community, giving rise to fears that the so-called 'beast', a monster enshrined in local legend, has returned to wreak fresh havoc. Kersey ventures up onto the moors to investigate and does not return - so Wycliffe is forced to go it alone. Jack Shepherd and Jimmy Yuill star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin are called to investigate when a man is attacked and his kidney is surgically removed. The focus quickly turns to finding the intended recipient of the organ, and the team is horrified to discover that it has been trafficked into the NHS and given to wealthy patient Joanna Woodleigh. When the case is handed over to the CPS, a conflict of interests becomes apparent as Steel and Phillips go up against George Castle in court. Denis Lawson guest stars, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate the death of middle-class teenager Debbie Powell. They discover the victim had been living a secret life as a drugs mule, and was being led astray by local dealer Gerry Craig. The case has an emotional impact on Castle, who demands Steel and Phillips do everything in their power to bring the person responsible to justice. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Bill Paterson star

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Toyah and Emily are stunned when Spider tells them he is moving on to a protest in Kent. Gail does not like the idea of Nick sharing a house with Zoe

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Ever since a beautiful American woman arrived in the peaceful village of Wychwood-under-Ashe, people have begun to die in a series of apparent accidents. Nobody suspects a killer is at work but, as the body count rises, Miss Marple digs a little deeper with the help of a former policeman - and they gradually unearth a secret that will rock the village to its core. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, Benedict Cumberbatch, David Haig, Sylvia Syms, Margo Stilley and Hugo Speer

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: An ancient Cornish folk ritual ends in the murder of a local undertaker - and provides quick-witted copper Charles Wycliffe with one of the most baffling mysteries of his career. Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters, Jimmy Yuill and Susan Penhaligon star

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway appears to have got over his fear of Lewis treading over his newly promoted toes. Their opposites have attracted in the past, and there seems no reason to suspect that they won't now the younger man has got used to the idea of his former mentor coming out of retirement to lend him a hand. Even DS Maddox isn't getting on Hathaway's nerves quite as much, so this rejuvenated trio sets out to discover who murdered American classics student Rose Anderson, whose body has been hauled from the canal with neck and abdomen wounds. Nevertheless, a few red herrings threaten to get in their way of landing the killer. Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox and Angela Griffin star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks agrees to help old friend and partner Jimmy Valentine with an undercover drug sting, but when the bust goes wrong and a dealer ends up dead, suspicion falls on the officers. As Devlin pushes for the truth, the spotlight turns on Brooks' dark past, and a 10-year-old case involving no witnesses and a large quantity of missing heroin. Guest starring Hustle's Robert Glenister, former Rising Damp star Don Warrington and Anna Chancellor (Four Weddings and a Funeral), alongside Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Suspicion falls on a pagan sect steeped in ancient beliefs when a baby is found dead. programme.title: Hornblower
programme.desc: Conclusion. Despite his reservations, Hornblower is reluctant to dishonour his bride-to-be by leaving her standing at the altar. However, with the ink barely dry on the marriage certificate, a surprise guest at the wedding breakfast despatches the groom to France on a mission to uncover the whereabouts of a missing sloop and its captain, Bracegirdle. En route, Hornblower rescues two strangers caught in a storm. Historical Navy drama, starring Ioan Gruffudd, Robert Lindsay, Paul McGann and Lorcan Cranitch

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade heads to a remote river in the Australian Outback in a bid to catch the rare and fearsome freshwater sawfish, which is reputed to reach 18ft long and attack boats. In his quest to discover more about the predator, he roams across an ancient landscape, encountering scientists and a real-life Crocodile Dundee, before finally confronting the disturbing truth

programme.title: The Horse Soldiers (1959)
programme.desc: A Union cavalry officer is ordered to demolish a railway junction in the middle of Confederate territory, but finds his authority constantly challenged by a military surgeon assigned to accompany him. John Ford's Western, based on an actual Civil War incident, starring John Wayne, William Holden, Constance Towers, Hoot Gibson and Anna Lee

Alex Horne helps coax the contestants as they attempt to complete Greg Davies' tasks

programme.title: The Circle
programme.desc: The latest twists and turns in the penultimate edition of the reality show, as a number of individuals continue to communicate over social media system, hoping to be voted the most popular and walk off the with the £100,000 prize

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: DCI Adam Gallop believes a man declared missing in 2015 has been murdered - but has so far been unable to locate his body, and the more time passes, the less chance there is of new witnesses or forensic evidence turning up. The police investigation takes officers from an urgent excavation in the woods where the missing man is believeds to have been shot to a journey across Europe in search of clues

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: A look at what happened after Gordon Ramsay headed to Scottsdale, Arizona, where he met husband-and-wife bistro owners Samy and Amy Bouzaglo, who revealed a shocking contempt for customer feedback. The chef faced the biggest challenge of the series to save Amy's Baking Company - and a media furore unfolded after the episode was broadcast

programme.title: Ramy
programme.desc: While still in Egypt, Ramy makes a gruelling journey across the desert to visit his grandfather. During the trek, things between him and his cousin Amani get considerably more complicated than either of them expected. Comedy drama, starring Ramy Youssef

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart tracks down and befriends a former Springfield Elementary student after Groundskeeper Willie reveals that he pulled off the 'ultimate prank'. programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Gloucestershire, married couple Paul and Debbie kick off the competition with a menu of tried and tested dinner party favourites. Perfectionists Angela and John cook up dishes inspired by their favourite restaurant meals. Finally, Kirsty and James plan an organic, locally sourced menu. At the table there's a lesson in asparagus eating and the hosts get a dose of their own medicine when their guests return their food criticism

programme.title: 50 Years of the Troubles: A Journey Through Film
programme.desc: Documentary marking the half century since sectarian riots erupted across Northern Ireland, widely seen as the beginning of the 30-year conflict known as The Troubles. Film maker Mark Cousins, who left Belfast at 18, returns to his home town to reflect on how the place and its history have been used, and occasionally abused, by cinema

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel suspects that an 18-month-old Australian Shepherd dog may be suffering from nerve compression, and an 11-month-old pug shows signs of neurological damage. It transpires that she has bone malformation that is squashing her spinal cord. Elsewhere, there is an emergency admission when a Jack Russell puppy called Bella is rushed in with a crushed pelvis after a freak accident

programme.title: The £100k Drop
programme.desc: Married couples Essex and Aberdeenshire take part in the quiz in which contestants lose some, or all, of the prize money with each wrong answer. Hosted by Davina McCall

programme.title: Food Unwrapped

On the final day, jokey couple Marc and Lou cook up a quirky menu of egg and bacon soup and chocolate garden pots. Maggie is put off by the soup and Tina isn't a fan of the lamb rump main, but it's Marc's low energy and seriousness that concerns the guests most of all

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Nick Hewer and Rachel Riley present the words-and-numbers game, with Nicki Chapman joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

After Lynne's husband Frankie was diagnosed with a terminal illness, Lynne took early retirement to care for him. A holiday home in the sun has been a lifelong dream for this couple, and with Frankie's illness they don't want to wait any longer. On the Cote d'Azur, the future of a new cookery school hangs in the balance. The owners of a home furnishing business look for a new challenge, and a yoga teacher from Somerset finally tastes sweet success in southern Spain

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa and Bart head to the seaside in the hope of finding treasure that has washed ashore during a storm. Meanwhile, Homer receives a huge electricity bill and takes drastic action to prevent it from happening again

programme.title: Hollyoaks

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Trip Hazard: My Great British Adventure
programme.desc: Comedian Rosie Jones embarks on an adventure around Britain, gorging on culture, learning local traditions and witnessing unique sights. In this first episode, Rosie takes TV presenter Scarlett Moffatt to the Lake District. The duo visit the former home of William Wordsworth to seek inspiration for their own poetry, before re-enacting a Viking battle at Kendal Castle. They also manage to survive a driving course on a 100-year-old traction engine, meet a historian to learn about the region's ancient cuisine and run into a spot of bother with a very old sausage-making machine. Narrated by Olivia Colman, with Rachel Stubbings

After being single for 24 years, retired dock worker Albert hopes thrill-seeking great-grandmother Carol will be impressed by his yacht, and airline flight dispatcher Ollie thinks he has found the perfect match in cancer nurse Debs when it turns out they are both love knitting and baking

programme.desc: The culinary contest arrives in Hull as five more diners hope to claim the grand prize of £1,000. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Wedding stationery entrepreneur Katrena hosts the third dinner party in Hull, and hopes to impress her guests with a cruise-ship-themed night featuring a pink strawberry soup starter, a sparkling lemon iceberg dessert and towel-folding entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: The £100k Drop

programme.desc: Ratings for Krusty's TV show start to fall, so executives bring in co-star Princess Penelope (voiced by Anne Hathaway) to increase its appeal, and romance soon blossoms between the clown and his new sidekick. programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to The Cardinal's Hat in Worcester, where owner Nigel Smith has decades of hospitality experience, but Mark and Jo are concerned by the condition of the bedding

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Third to host this week are husband and wife duo Russ and Karen Colman, who hope their glamping site in Cledan Valley, mid-Wales, will win over their rivals

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Lucinda Renshaw welcomes the guests to Springfield House B&B in Instow, Devon, where things get off to a good start - until one couple realise they do not have a lock on their door

programme.title: Four in a Bed

The Cimon is first and hosts Mark and Jo want to question Karen and Russ about their breakfast feedback. Next, Nigel from The Cardinal's Hat wants clarity on some cleaning and hygiene comments made by Mark and Jo. Russ and Karen from Cledan Valley are third and are keen to put Mark and Jo straight. Finally, it's Springfield House and owner Lucinda and friend Diane want to put two of the guests at ease, but it's the payments where true feelings are expressed

Driven by the need to alleviate their two young sons' life-threatening allergies, their new part-submerged, shed-inspired home, will be constructed using low toxin materials and deploy cutting-edge filtration systems to clean the air they breathe. Will their innovative new house deliver the benefits Elinor and Born are hoping for?

Meanwhile, the castle owners the Hays, move into the other family's rented three-bedroom terrace on the outskirts of Exeter and are given a weekly budget of £150 - far less than the £1,050 they are used to. How will both families find their new surroundings?

programme.title: World's Most Scenic River Journeys

programme.title: Churchill
programme.desc: The story of Winston Churchill's return from the political wilderness to become the heroic war leader many still celebrate today. Though Churchill stood against India's move towards independence, his warnings about Nazi Germany were thought by some to be misplaced, and eventually he was proved right. In this episode, those closest to him reveal the true story behind the most important speech of his life

programme.title: Britain's Favourite 80s Songs
programme.desc: A look at the biggest-selling singles of 1985, the best pop videos, the artists who appeared in them, and the stories behind the songs. A-ha's Take On Me, and I Know Him So Well, by Elaine Paige and Barbara Dickson are two of the classic tracks under discussion. Contributors include Toyah Willcox, Paul Gambaccini, Cheryl Baker and Carol Decker. Jenny Powell narrates

programme.title: That's So
programme.desc: An entertaining look back at events in 1985, using news, pop and TV archive clips to provide a nostalgic reminder of the music, fashions and news events of the time, including the Live Aid concerts in London and Philadelphia prompted by the famine in Ethiopia. The year also saw riots erupting in Tottenham, north London, while the world of football witnessed two major tragedies at the Heysel Stadium in Brussels and Bradford City's Valley Parade ground. The year saw political change in the Soviet Union when Mikhail Gorbachev became leader, while Dire Straits became the first band to have their music video aired on MTV, and Clive Sinclair launched his ill-fated battery-assisted tricycle, the Sinclair C5

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Crooked House
programme.desc: Adaptation of the mystery novel, starring Max Irons and Terence Stamp. Charles, the son of the assistant commissioner at Scotland Yard returns to London from Cairo following the end of the Second World War, only to discover his fiancee's grandfather has been murdered. He uses his father's connections to become involved in the investigation, and finds the dead man's second wife a likely suspect. However, nobody in the household has an alibi, and Charles' fiancee may have the clearest motive for murder. With Gillian Anderson and Glenn Close

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Crooked House
programme.desc: Adaptation of the mystery novel, starring Max Irons and Terence Stamp. Charles, the son of the assistant commissioner at Scotland Yard returns to London from Cairo following the end of the Second World War, only to discover his fiancee's grandfather has been murdered. He uses his father's connections to become involved in the investigation, and finds the dead man's second wife a likely suspect. programme.title: 5 News Weekend
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.title: Susan Calman's Grand Day Out
programme.desc: The comedian aboard her vintage camper van, explores the Pembroke coast in Wales. She begins her 130-mile road trip just north of Pembrokeshire near the town of Cardigan, then follows the coastline south to Carmarthenshire, ending her trip at Pendine Sands. Her first visit is to the Teifi River where she's invited to paddle an unusually small boat called a coracle, then it's on to the Blue Lagoon for a spot of cliff jumping. Next, Susan samples Welsh seaweed delicacy laverbread at Broadhaven Beach, before visiting the home of the celebrated Welsh poet Dylan Thomas

programme.title: Jane McDonald's Weekends Away
programme.desc: The singer embarks on short weekend breaks in Europe, the UK and the USA, and shares her pick of the best destinations for a short break. In Valetta, the historic 16th-century capital city of Malta, Jane spends a couple of days soaking up the culture and visiting the famous sites. She starts her visit with a water-taxi tour around the bay, and then joins a private walking tour around the heart of the old city, including the fountain, main entrance gates, amphitheatre and finishing at St John's Co-cathedral

programme.title: Edward and Sophie: The Reluctant Royals
programme.desc: A profile of the Earl and Countess of Wessex, a couple who often go below the radar, but are becoming increasingly popular and significant in the royal family. Prince Edward will one day will take on the mantle of Duke of Edinburgh from his father, along with many of his duties, while Sophie has become admired for her charity work and as one of the Queen's closest confidantes

programme.title: Edward VII: The Playboy Prince Who Changed Britain
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the king's life and reign, looking at the relationships with the women in his life and considering the remarkable period in history he presided over - an era when the arts, culture, technology and architecture flourished in Britain

programme.title: Britain's Favourite Comedy
programme.desc: Jo Brand presents a celebration of great sketches of the 1970s, 80s and 90s, with each show featuring archive material and contributions from the writers and performers themselves, beginning with selection from the 1970s. From Morecambe and Wise's breakfast to Monty Python and an infamous (and lifeless) feathered friend, Jo explores just what made these sketches so good, while Barry Cryer shares his memories of working with The Two Ronnies, and Charlie Higson remembering how Dick Emery inspired The Fast Show

programme.title: Jason Manford Live at the Manchester Apollo
programme.desc: The comedian performs before his home-town audience in Manchester during his 2009 tour, featuring observational story-telling, anecdotes and teasing repartee with the audience. Among the topics he offers his thoughts on are the rules of driving, the lies dads tell, the proper way to use a urinal and the problems of dealing with nappies

programme.title: Tattoo Disasters UK
programme.desc: A former soldier wants to get rid of the swearword on his shoulder before his two children start asking awkward questions

programme.title: The Funny Thing About
programme.desc: Johnny Vegas presents a whistle-stop tour of awkward, embarrassing and cringeworthy holiday moments, as related by a selection of comedians and celebrities. Johnny's famous contributors skewer the traditional staycation, questioning the perks of rain-drenched British beaches, and whether anyone really likes going camping. Turning their attention further afield, the stars share some of their most memorable holiday tales, including transforming a hotel corridor into one long 'slip-and-slide', the suspicious discovery of a pair of chicken fillets in a swimming pool in Marbella, and a blush-inducing incident involving a naked paragliding session along a Spanish beachfront

programme.title: Nick's Quest
programme.desc: Nick Baker highlights the plight of South American Orinoco crocodiles as he documents a conservation project in Venezuela to save the species from extinction. In addition to heading out on a dangerous night-time hunt to find out how the creatures are surviving in the wild, he tries to coax a three-year-old to return to the open river

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Ann Maurice and Tris Payne visit Southampton's prestigious marina development Ocean Village, where a couple's home needs sprucing up to attract potential buyers after being on the market for three months

programme.title: Horton Hears a Who! (2008)
programme.desc: A kindly elephant hears a cry of help coming from a speck of dust and discovers the minuscule city of Whoville. As he tries to help its citizens, the other animals begin to question his sanity, and the evil Kangaroo incites them to turn against the do-gooder. Animated adaptation of Dr Seuss's book, with the voices of Jim Carrey, Steve Carell, Carol Burnett, Seth Rogen and Will Arnett

programme.title: The SpongeBob Movie: Sponge Out of Water (2015)
programme.desc: The cheery sea creature tries to prevent a precious secret formula from falling into the wrong hands - but when it goes missing, the end of the world approaches. SpongeBob and his friends embark on a quest to get it back, which leads to them developing superpowers and venturing onto dry land. Animated comedy, with the voices of Tom Kenny and Antonio Banderas

programme.title: Two by Two (2015)
programme.desc: Two strange creatures and their children are barred from getting on board Noah's Ark, but manage to sneak in. When the kids fall overboard, they face a struggle for survival as the flood waters rise, while their distraught parents must work together to rescue them. Animated adventure, with the voices of Tara Flynn and Dermot Magennis

programme.title: Teen Wolf (1985)
programme.desc: A teenage misfit is initially dismayed to learn he is a werewolf as a result of a hereditary curse. He finds there are advantages as he becomes a local celebrity, while his enhanced agility makes him the star player of his school's basketball team - but fame quickly goes to his head. Comedy, starring Michael J Fox, James Hampton and Susan Ursitti

programme.title: Transformers: The Last Knight (2017)
programme.desc: The shape-changing robots and their human allies face a confrontation with their original creator, who has brainwashed their leader and intends to save her own home planet by destroying Earth. To save the human race they must recover an ancient artefact from Arthurian legend - but their mission is threatened by old enemies as they uncover a terrible secret about the history of the world. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Mark Wahlberg, Anthony Hopkins, Gemma Chan, Stanley Tucci and John Turturro

programme.title: Commando (1985)
programme.desc: The daughter of retired special forces officer John Matrix is kidnapped by an old comrade-in-arms, now in league with a deposed former dictator. The tyrant plans to use the girl to force Matrix into assassinating the head of the country he once ruled, but is unprepared for the lengths to which the veteran soldier will go to rescue his child. Action adventure, with Arnold Schwarzenegger and Rae Dawn Chong

programme.title: Overlord (2018)
programme.desc: In 1944, a group of American soldiers land in an occupied French hamlet after their plane is destroyed by enemy fire. Their mission is to take out a heavily fortified Nazi radio tower hours before D-Day. However, they stumble across a mysterious Nazi lab where their bloodthirsty enemies are waiting for them. Action thriller from producer JJ Abrams, starring Jovan Adepo, Wyatt Russell and Mathilde Ollivier

programme.title: The Raid 2 (2014)
programme.desc: A cop who survived fighting his way through a drug lord's lair is reassigned to undercover duties. He is sent to infiltrate a crime syndicate and expose a connection between the gangsters and corruption on the force, but his personal vendetta threatens to disrupt the mission. Action thriller sequel, starring Iko Uwais and Arifin Putra. In Indonesian, Japanese, English and Arabic

programme.title: Hotel for Dogs (2009)
programme.desc: An orphaned brother and sister face losing their beloved dog when they go into a foster home that does not allow pets. The kids find the ideal hiding place in an abandoned hotel, and set about transforming it into luxury canine accommodation for not just their beloved pet, but every stray in town. Family comedy, starring Emma Roberts, Jake T Austin, Don Cheadle and Lisa Kudrow

programme.title: Flight of the Navigator (1986)
programme.desc: A boy mysteriously disappears in 1978, and is found eight years later, having not aged a day. He escapes being experimented on by government scientists by taking off in a crashed alien spaceship. With the help of the robot pilot, he tries to find out what has happened to him and if he can get back home. Sci-fi adventure, starring Joey Cramer and Veronica Cartwright, with the voice of Paul Reubens

programme.title: Shrek Forever After (2010)
programme.desc: The grumpy ogre longs for his simple life as a feared monster, so strikes a deal with magical trickster Rumpelstiltskin to get back to the way things were. However, he soon finds there is a catch - he ends up stuck in an alternate world where none of his friends know who he is and where Rumpelstiltskin has become the kingdom's tyrannical ruler. Animated comedy, with the voices of Mike Myers, Eddie Murphy and Cameron Diaz

programme.title: We Bought a Zoo (2011)
programme.desc: A father-of-two struggling to cope with the death of his wife is in need of a fresh start, so buys a house with a dilapidated zoo on the grounds. With the help of the keepers, he works to restore the attraction to its former glory, while also trying to reconnect with his troubled teenage son. Fact-based comedy drama, starring Matt Damon, Scarlett Johansson and Thomas Haden Church

programme.title: Suffragette (2015)
programme.desc: A woman joins a campaign to gain the right to vote in 1912. She and her allies find their peaceful protests accomplishing little in the face of an unwavering state, so resort to increasingly violent measures, risking everything they have in the pursuit of political justice. Period drama, starring Carey Mulligan, Helena Bonham Carter, Anne-Marie Duff and Meryl Streep

programme.title: Kingsman: The Golden Circle (2017)
programme.desc: Having saved the world once, secret agents Eggsy and Merlin join forces with their US counterparts to bring down a psychotic femme fatale. Thankfully they are assisted by a veteran hero brought back from the dead, and cross paths with a kidnapped Elton John and a couple of killer robot dogs along the way. Director Matthew Vaughn's comedy adventure sequel, starring Taron Egerton, Colin Firth, Mark Strong and Julianne Moore

programme.title: Ted 2 (2015)
programme.desc: The foul-mouthed talking teddy bear is horrified to discover that he counts as property according to the law. He and his human best friend hire a pot-smoking lawyer to help them fight a court case and get Ted legally recognised as a person. Comedy sequel, starring Mark Wahlberg and Amanda Seyfried, with the voice of Seth MacFarlane

programme.title: Franklyn (2008)
programme.desc: Alternative worlds collide as the fates of four lost souls are intertwined across parallel universes. A man searches for his lost son in modern-day London, a masked vigilante pursues a murderous cult leader in the gothic metropolis of Meanwhile City, an artist's obsession with suicide takes a dangerous turn and a heartbroken man seeks a route back to the purity of his first love. Fantasy drama, starring Ryan Phillippe, Eva Green, Sam Riley, Bernard Hill and Art Malik

programme.title: The Rugrats Movie (1998)
programme.desc: Tommy tries to return his new brother to the baby shop, helped by Angelica, Chuckie, Phil and Lil - but the friends become lost in dense woodlands en route, turning their expedition into a scary and exciting journey of discovery. Animated comedy based on the TV series, with the voices of Elizabeth Daily, Christine Cavanaugh, Kath Soucie, Whoopi Goldberg and Busta Rhymes

programme.title: Alpha (2018)
programme.desc: Europe, 20,000 years ago, and a young man is left for dead while hunting with his tribe. After forming an alliance with a lone wolf that was abandoned by its pack, the duo attempts to make their way to safety. Prehistoric adventure, starring Kodi Smit-McPhee and Jóhannes Haukur Jóhannesson. Directed by Albert Hughes (The Book of Eli)

programme.title: Mrs Doubtfire (1993)
programme.desc: A struggling actor's ambitious, no-nonsense wife throws him out and demands a divorce. Desperate to spend time with his children, he puts his talents to good use by donning drag and posing as a British housekeeper to get a job looking after them. Comedy, starring Robin Williams, Sally Field, Pierce Brosnan, Harvey Fierstein and Mara Wilson

programme.title: Leap Year (2010)
programme.desc: A woman plans to take advantage of an Irish tradition to propose to her boyfriend while on holiday in Dublin. When they become separated, she faces a disastrous journey across the country in the company of a charming local innkeeper, and is tempted to leave her boyfriend for her travelling companion. Romantic comedy, starring Amy Adams, Matthew Goode and Adam Scott

programme.title: Hampstead (2017)
programme.desc: Bored American widow Emily becomes involved with Donald, a gruff Irish loner living wild on Hampstead Heath, even though the worlds they inhabit could not be more different. When his home is threatened by greedy real estate developers, she believes she has found her new cause - but gets more than she bargained for when unexpected romance blossoms. Romantic comedy drama, starring Brendan Gleeson, Diane Keaton and James Norton

As he strives to clear his name, he must also fight his way back into the blazing tower to rescue his family, who are being held hostage by criminals 225 storeys up. Action thriller, starring Dwayne Johnson, Neve Campbell and Chin Han

Horror thriller, starring Joey King, Julia Goldani Telles and Jaz Sinclair

Fantasy based on the stories of Giambattista Basile, starring Salma Hayek, Vincent Cassel and Toby Jones

programme.desc: Kate Quilton visits the world's largest jelly bean producers to discover what they are made of instead of gelatine, and Matt Tebbutt finds out why maple syrup is so expensive

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: After years spent dedicated to their demanding careers in nursing and social work, Gillian and Paul Clarke from Stockport have decided it's time to start taking it a little easier and put themselves first. They've got their hearts set on a holiday home on Spain's Costa Cálida where they can boogie their way to retirement. Jonnie Irwin shows the couple five properties for their budget of £45,000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The team is in Hay-on-Wye and Bedford, where Henry restores a bespoke workbench, and Simon ponders the possibilities of some discarded planks of wood

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien restores an enamel roll-top bath and builds a den out of old doors. Henry Cole makes a wall mounted safe from a jerry can and lovingly restores a cheese-making trough

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate researchers try to solve the tricky case of a man named Smith. Plus, the story of a man whose troubled childhood led him to become separated from his adoptive family, and details of more unclaimed estates

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton visits West Yorkshire, where Emma and Martin are battling wind and snow while building a stone home on an imposing hilltop. They've self-built before and Martin works in the trade, but the sheer scale of the project and an escalating budget leave the couple scrambling to finish before their family face homelessness

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at the Bell House Hotel in Wiltshire, where business partners Chris Williams and Jacqui Hogg hope guests will appreciate their attempts to bring London chic to the shires. Susan and David Speak are frustrated by the low shower pressure, while Jade Hendon butts heads with Matthew over healthy eating at dinner and the hosts' absence at breakfast the next morning gets the criticism flowing

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the Partridge in Oxfordshire, where young couple Matthew Cockman and Paula Szkwarkowska hope guests will get on board with their passion for local organic food. A communal bathroom has the guests lost for words, and the lack of a hot breakfast included in the price leaves a bad taste in everyone's mouths

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Strawberry Gardens B&B in Heysham for the third day of the contest, where husband and wife team Susan and David Speak offer home-from-home accommodation and an abundance of northern hospitality. Sports enthusiast David treats his guests to a traditional darts competition, adjudicated by former world champion Alan Warriner-Little, but he is unimpressed when Paula sends back her evening meal. However, it is Chris and Jacqui's feedback that disappoints the hosts the most

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Bateman's Mill near Chesterfield, where manager Jade Hendon has high hopes of being the best. But she faces an uphill battle when David and Susan go out of their way to find hairs in the bathroom - and to make mattters worse, her Derbyshire breakfast is not a hit with Chris and Jacqui

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores, starting with Chris and Jacqui, owners of the Bell House, who want to find out why Matthew marked them down for being 'too tall' for his bed. The group members proceed to thrash out their differences before the week's winners are revealed

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo head to Wales to see how can create the most profit on items, including an oil dispenser, fire grate, chaise longue and even a catamaran

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: With numbers in the wild dwindling rapidly, Chester Zoo's black rhino breeding programme is a key part of preserving the species. Black rhino Kitani is introduced to new mate Magadi, but with injury a high risk during mating, keeper Babs is concerned that Kitani may struggle to defend herself, since she lost her horn after the death of her calf. Meanwhile, there's a surprise birth in the orangutan house, and older sibling Tuti has to fend for herself and set out on the road to independence

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge follows the fortunes of Brit Mandy and her American business partner Steve, who have decided to build a swimming pool to entice more paying guests to their stunning cliff-edge chateau in the Dordogne. Desperate to increase income at his chateau, Michael invites Angel Adoree to help out with an experiment to do afternoon tea for the locals

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Tim and Zoe Bawtree, who live in a costly Regency property in Cheltenham, Gloucestershire, plan to build a modern, low-maintenance house in their back garden. However, height restrictions mean 60 per cent of their new home has to be underground and there are 90 objections from neighbours to their planning application. Undeterred, the pair press ahead, only to encounter further problems - including the departure of the builders

programme.title: The Good Fight
programme.desc: A former divorce client seeks the firm's help, claiming a new play running in Chicago is based on his divorce and shares specific details that unearth more than just his dirty laundry. Diane tries to get to the heart of Memo 618. Legal drama, starring Christine Baranski, Delroy Lindo and Audra McDonald

programme.title: The Tudors
programme.desc: An envoy from the Papal States arrives to discuss the annulment of Henry's marriage to Catherine of Aragon. The outcome of the talks will determine Cardinal Wolsey's future and influence the nature of the relationship between England and Rome

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Five-year-old Isa is rushed to A&E after being hit by a car and trapped underneath it, and doctors suspect the impact may have caused serious internal damage, as well as significant head injuries. Meanwhile, 26-year-old Tom is treated after slicing through his leg with a circular saw, with medics fearing the blade has cut so deeply into the muscle tissue that it could starve his leg of blood. Plus, 85-year-old retired actor Donald comes to the hospital with pain in his bladder, and consultant Faheem is concerned there may be an infection

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts, with Sean Lock and Stephen Mangan taking on guest captain Richard Ayoade and Katherine Ryan. Bill Bailey is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent, while Rachel Riley is in charge of the letters and numbers

programme.title: New Amsterdam
programme.desc: Max races against the clock to rearrange the budget when faced with employees going unpaid, while Kapoor is presented with a device that's the future of medicine. Medical drama, starring Ryan Eggold and Anupam Kher

programme.title: The Good Fight
programme.desc: A former divorce client seeks the firm's help, claiming a new play running in Chicago is based on his divorce and shares specific details that unearth more than just his dirty laundry. Diane tries to get to the heart of Memo 618. Legal drama, starring Christine Baranski, Delroy Lindo and Audra McDonald

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Mauritius to find out what the difference is between white and dark rum, and learns the explosive origins of the term proof

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: After becoming foster parents to three young children, Jo Danson and her husband Bill decided a holiday home would be the perfect way for everyone to spend quality time together. Working within a budget of £65,000, Jasmine Harman shows Jo five suitable properties in the Orihuela Costa region of Spain

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Herefordshire and Worcestershire. Henry makes a table from the base of a broken agricultural grain mill, while Simon creates a garden seat from an old mangle

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The team visits Swindon and Chippenham, where Henry Cole finds and restores a much-maligned BSA Ariel 3 moped and Simon O'Brien gives a Ford Transit gearbox a new lease of life

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate researchers investigate a huge case that has them searching far and wide for rightful heirs. They also successfully reunite a family separated by wartime evacuation. Plus, details of unclaimed estates

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton assists Christine and Frank in rural Cambridgeshire. The couple are determined to build a huge barn-style home fit for big family get-togethers. The problem is, they don't appreciate the sheer scale of the undertaking until it's too late - leaving Frank facing a workload grossly exceeding the original plan

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at David and Ewa Woodward's Keats Cottage in Shanklin on the Isle of Wight. In the rooms, Donna Taylor from Jasmine House in Leicestershire makes a hair-raising discovery, while mother-and-daughter team Lesley and Chloe Priestly take a dislike to one of their competitors at dinner

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Jasmine House in Lutterworth, Leicestershire managed by Donna Taylor who believes in a no frills approach. But it doesn't take long for Martin and Sara to realise all the added extras in the room don't add up. During the afternoon Leslie and Chloe show their fighting spirit during a kickboxing lesson and all the B&Bers square up to their host

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the luxury Halliwell House in Cheshire for the third day of the contest, where owners Martin Hulme and Sara Williams charge nearly £500 per night. Donna is astonished at the room rates, while David and Ewa are expecting nothing but perfection, but the self-service approach at breakfast confuses the guests, leaving Martin and Sara defiant in the face of their feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final two days see the contestants staying the night at Soar Chapel Guest House in Ebbw Vale, the family home of mum and daughter Lesley and Chloe Priestly. Expectations are high for this duo and one guest in particular is not disappointed. Things get going in the afternoon during a beatboxing contest, although old tensions resurface at dinner and the hosts are left to calm things down. A massive spread is laid on at breakfast, but when one guest takes issue with the cooked items, Lesley and Chloe worry what impact it will have come payment day

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. David and Ewa Woodward want to challenge Martin's criticism over the way they run their business. Next, Donna Taylor wants to find out more about the issue that caused Martin and Sara to lose sleep. Needless to say, there is tension in the air and axes to grind, before the winner is revealed

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien attempt to restore items including an old cheese press, and a 1980s motorbike almost gets the better of Henry and his restorer Guy

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Florence and Nadine, two of Chester's Grevy's zebras, are about to give birth, but other animals are struggling to produce offspring, including Iblis the Asiatic lion. Meanwhile, warthog dad Magnum is feeling the pressure of parenting as two of his older children, Dobby and Neville, are becoming increasingly hard to control, and Magnum is losing his hair with the stress

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: As Jonathan and Michael prepare their chateau for the big wedding, they have a sewage problem that threatens to take the whole first floor out of action; with 40 guests due, the timing couldn't be worse. Mandy and Steve's pool problems continue, and, with the first guests of the season about to arrive, they also have to contend with a collapsed wall. It's decision time for Philip and Angelina - can they find their own chateau to buy or is it time to return to Britain?

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Architect Martin Pease plans an unusual home for his family where everything is white and open plan. He calls upon his experience of designing commercial buildings to build the house and uses materials and systems more commonly used in office projects. The biggest risk is that his house may look more like a car showroom than a comfortable retreat

programme.title: Deutschland 89
programme.desc: Desperate to prevent German reunification, Lenora seeks Fuchs' help in Italy, home of the HVA in exile. Fuchs tries to find a peaceful solution between his former agents, but Martin and Lenora have other ideas. Tina receives a mysterious visitor, while Martin is growing suspicious of Nicole's true intentions. In German

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Ten-year-old Ashleigh is rushed to St George's by ambulance after twisting her leg while playing football. X-rays reveal that her femur is broken and she will need surgery. Meanwhile, three-year-old Lola visits A&E with her dad after having a seizure, and 91-year-old Betty arrives with severe chest pain. Doctors carry out a blood test in order to find out whether she is having a heart attack and will require life-saving surgery

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Barrie, 78, is rushed in with breathing difficulties and tests reveal he has fluid on his lungs and faces serious medical treatment, while 75-year-old David is treated after falling down a flight of stairs, and 82-year-old Bob is admitted after a DIY accident at home

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and-numbers quiz, with Jon Richardson and Cariad Lloyd taking on guest captain Alan Carr and Kevin Bridges. Elis James and John Robins join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Ten-year-old Ashleigh is rushed to St George's by ambulance after twisting her leg while playing football. X-rays reveal that her femur is broken and she will need surgery. Meanwhile, three-year-old Lola visits A&E with her dad after having a seizure, and 91-year-old Betty arrives with severe chest pain. programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty looks into skyr, a mysterious yoghurt-like product that claims to be entirely fat-free, but has a surprisingly creamy texture, and Kate Quilton heads to one of the busiest oyster hatcheries in France, where she witnesses the amazing science that goes into providing oysters year-round. Matt Tebbutt wants to know if the dried strawberries in his cereal are real or just a clever imitation

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Italy to assess why Parmesan costs up to twice as much as supermarket cheddar and Kate Quilton travels to Hungary and Spain to find out exactly where paprika comes from. Plus, Matt Tebbutt looks at the best and quickest ways to remove the shell from a hard-boiled egg

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Birmingham pair Derek Hansel and Gina Awoko are looking for a home in Almeria, Spain, that they can eventually retire to and also generate some rental income from. Jasmine Harman shows them a range of properties within their £300,000 budget, from a coastal renovation project to an inland cortijo. However, it remains to be seen whether she can deliver the perfect property in the perfect location

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Having sold a successful business, Ruth and Philip Trinder from Northamptonshire want to buy an apartment on the Balearic island of Majorca, where they can spend half the year. They want somewhere they can entertain family and friends, within easy walking distance of one of the island's stylish ports. With a budget of £300,000 Laura Hamilton sets out to help them find something with the contemporary look they love

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Debra Mann from Reading, Berkshire, has fallen in love with the Bay of the Somme in the north of France, and has set heart on a holiday getaway where she and her sister can spend time with family and friends. Scarlette Douglas shows them both a selection of properties for a £64,000 budget - with plenty of surprises along the way

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer are house-hunting for two couples who are keen to get on the property ladder in Swindon. Kirstie meets Candice and Alistair, who are looking to move out of Alistair's bachelor pad and into a family home as they are expecting the arrival of their first baby. Meanwhile, Phil is in the countryside between Swindon and Newbury where he meets James and Hayley, who are looking for a rural retreat for themselves and their two dogs

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dry-cleaning manager Valerie hosts the first dinner party in Lancashire, where she plans to impress her guests with a lamb hotpot and trifle. However, it remains to be seen whether her warm-hearted hospitality will be enough to win her the £1,000 prize by the end of the week

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dance teacher Nicky hosts the second night in Lancashire, where she hopes to impress her guests with a round-the-world themed menu inspired by her dancing tours, which includes a Chinese salmon starter, and an American dessert. However, with fellow contestant Peggy born in the United States, the host may have her work cut out in presenting the perfect dish

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Outsourcing manager Steve hosts the third night in Lancashire, where the 51-year-old plans to wow his guests with a 'man of the world' menu that relies heavily on strong Indian spices. Peggy picks him up on his shop-bought black pudding, while Nicky is unimpressed by the mildness of the chicken balti, resulting in a humiliating chilli-sauce challenge

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: American Peggy hosts a USA-themed dinner party on the fourth night of the competition in Lancashire, and hopes her Arizonan recipes will be enough to win her the £1,000 prize. Her menu comprises tried and tested dishes, including her dessert of peach dump cake. However, friction between her and fellow contestant Steve continues to build

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the final dinner party in Lancashire, and the turn of sales manager Andy to host. He is confident in his locally sourced gastro menu and delivers a meal with plenty of flair, but faces the challenge of impressing foodie Nicky. He is also under pressure to convince American Peggy of the merits of the region's food, while his fellow Lancastrians Valerie and Steve are keen to see how the area has influenced his cooking

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Steve and Beverly Green welcome their guests to Centery Farm, Devon, where one contestant decides to keep her past as a hotel inspector secret. The couple's afternoon herding activity is thwarted by heavy rain and fog, and the following morning's feedback showdown has repercussions that will be felt all week

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Professional chef Ben Cooke invites the contestants to the Little Gloster on the Isle of Wight, where he hopes to impress them with a taste of Scandinavian living. Assisted by his 81-year-old grandmother Lillian, who has more than 50 years' experience in the hotel industry, the host splits the visitors into two teams for a contest, but simmering tensions quickly reach boiling-point, resulting in a bitter snub at dinner

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Stephen Hammon hosts the third day of the competition at the four-star Old Hall House B&B in Warwickshire, providing his guests with a variety of on-site activities including shooting and archery before taking them for a meal at his own restaurant. However, he ends up trying too hard to impress at breakfast the next day after Sandie De Rougemont reveals her past as a hotel inspector

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Ex-hotel inspector Sandie De Rougemont, owner of the House of Agnes in Canterbury, plays host. She takes her guests to a local brewery and an upmarket restaurant, where everyone seems to enjoy themselves, but the revelation that a ghost may roam the rooms of the B&B leaves some of the participants nervous

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programme.desc: Matt calls in old family friend Richard and his heavy machinery to take on his biggest plan to date - a new road around the farm, hopefully making everything more accessible for his parents. The list of repairs keeps growing and Matt finds himself in a race against the rain to fix a hole in the roof of the new chicken barn, but the family get a welcome break from work when the puppies are born and Molly, Luke and Nicola get to meet the new arrivals for the first time

programme.title: Walking with Elephants
programme.desc: Levison Wood reaches the final stage of his epic 650-mile journey, following the annual migration of Botswana's 130,000 elephants. Together with his guide Kane, Lev follows the elephants deep into the heart of the Okavango Delta, a vast wetland teeming with wildlife where every year more than 40,000 elephants gather to feed. Lev and Kane narrowly avoid a pride of lions who have brought down a male buffalo and watch as they move in for the kill, but are forced to wade through wide channels where crocodiles, hippos and snakes lurk

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Doctors carry out tests on 77-year-old Martin, who was rushed to St George's after collapsing at home and suffered two further seizures in A&E. As he waits to find out what is causing him problems, his wife Margaret talks about their life together. Meanwhile, 83-year-old widower Harry arrives with shortness of breath, chest pains and dizziness after collapsing while shopping. Paediatric nurse Kelly, who loves working with children, treats six-year-old Lilly, who has cut her head after falling off her scooter

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Thirty-seven-year-old Robert arrives at St George's Hospital, London after colliding with a car on his motorbike. Scans show he has a bleed on the brain, a broken jaw, and two missing teeth, and he faces potentially life-changing injuries and extensive surgery. Meanwhile, 54-year-old Mary collapsed in the street on the way to work and hit her head. Doctors need to establish whether it is simple fainting, or whether something more sinister is to blame, with tests revealing she will need a pacemaker fitted. Plus, 27-year-old Jessica comes to A&E with a wound that will not heal, after having a lumpectomy to remove breast cancer tissue two months ago

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and-numbers quiz, with guest captain Kathy Burke and Roisin Conaty taking on guest captain Johnny Vegas and Bob Mortimer. John Cooper Clarke joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Ted struggles with a crush he has for a lady visitor, and the arrival of a group of nuns does not help matters. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon and Frank Kelly, with a guest appearance by Gemma Craven

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Jack is declared dead after drinking too much floor polish - and Ted is astounded to learn he has been left a fortune in the will. However, he has to fulfil certain conditions before collecting the money

programme.desc: The Alvarado brothers stumble across an interesting collectible, while Brandon and Lori bid on a storage facility for pets

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Jesse and the Alvarado brothers get devious, while a frustrated Brandon and Lori look as if they might end up leaving the auction empty-handed

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys take to the track for a grudge match drag race, with Mike and Frank both determined to prove they have the fastest ride

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys stumble on a mountain of motorbikes. However, as they soon discover, getting to them is going to be far from easy

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race
programme.desc: Elysian Park in Los Angeles is turned into an obstacle-littered course for the day as 70 teams of adrenaline junkies put their gravity-powered contraptions to the test

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters try to make an ordinary second-hand car lap the test track as fast as a new, purpose-built sports saloon. James May meets former Formula 1 World Champion Mika Hakkinen to discover why Finns are such good racing drivers, Richard Hammond tests the Veritas sports car, and actor Mark Wahlberg is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters are challenged to drive from Switzerland to Blackpool on just one tank of fuel, and the winner gets to switch on the Blackpool illuminations. James May also power-tests the Pagani Zonda F Roadster, and the Stig sets a lap time in the Bugatti Veyron. Comedian Harry Enfield is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Berlin, where history and the avant garde dwell side by side. A city that once made history with its divisive wall and Checkpoint Charlie now teems with an overwhelming array of innovative chefs offering delicious seasonal creations, such as pureed sunflower seeds, Jerusalem artichoke flans and crushed frozen pine nuts. Loved by the likes of Greta Garbo, Charlie Chaplin and Auguste Escoffier there's still a cornucopia of choice for those with a more traditional temperament with eisbein, meatballs and sausages topping every menu in the German capital

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond celebrates the 40th anniversary of the Ferrari Daytona by taking one back to its spiritual home - the Italian Riviera - before trying to beat James May and a powerboat in a race from Portofino to St Tropez. Back in the UK, Jeremy Clarkson tests the BMW M3 saloon and compares it with the Lexus IS-F. Grand Designs presenter Kevin McCloud is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May ask whether the Eastern Bloc countries ever made a good car, and Richard Hammond assesses the performance of German sports model the Veritas. The Ford Fiesta is also put through its paces in a stringent road test, and Mayor of London Boris Johnson is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Aisling Bea. Bob Mortimer, Mark Watson, Nish Kumar and Sally Philips take part in a series of bizarre tasks, with Greg Davies ultimately deciding who wins. Mark Watson tries to open an unlocked briefcase

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee wants to create the perfect Christmas for Lucy, so he invites her to spend the holiday in a cosy old country house that used to belong to his dear, dead old aunt - and to make it a proper family get-together, he invites along her parents and dipsy friend Daisy. But the Christmas spirit is in short supply when Lucy discovers that the cottage has not been lived in for years - and it isn't long before they have sensed a spooky presence. Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix star in this festive special of the sitcom, with Bobby Ball, Geoffrey Whitehead and Deborah Grant. Festive edition from 2013

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee Mack's not having much luck with lights these days, whether it's the fizzling sign at the start of this show, the blackout during his recent Sheffield gig, or the dimly lit area at the start of this seventh series. Lee and Lucy have enjoyed a spot of culture at their local cinema, but the sparkle of a French movie is soon diminished when the romantic walk home finds them in an imposing underpass. She has her handbag stolen by a bunch of cocky youths from under his nose. Given the fact the layabout failed to protect her, or come up with an accurate description of their muggers, Lee feels the urge to prove his manliness, but will a spell at the local boxing ring be a help or a hindrance? Sally Bretton and Katy Wix also star

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: A wealthy new couple move into the block and soon find out that sharing a building with Lee and Lucy is anything but straightforward. The fun begins when Lee somehow manages to bag an invitation to a christening party being thrown by the couple - sending him and Lucy on a last-minute gift-buying trip, during which they make every wrong decision humanly possible to find the perfect present for the baby who seems to have everything. Hugh Dennis and Abigail Cruttenden join the cast, with Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix

programme.title: Not Going Out

Then, Michael heads south into Nottinghamshire and to the former pit village of Eastwood, before arriving in Nantwich, where he takes a dip in the briny

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels between Liverpool and Wales. Along the way he discovers a childhood favourite - a miniature tank engine with three carriages in dark red - made by Frank Hornby, and learns the story of a Welsh countess who lived in Gwrych Castle. In Snowdonia, he also rides the waves with a Welsh surfing champion

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In picturesque Snowdonia, Michael Portillo braves the fastest zip line in the world - stretching 1,500 feet across a vast slate quarry as he begins the last leg of his journey to Caernarfon. En route, he also visits the home of British mountaineering, Pen y Gwryd, to hear how an Edwardian journalist and poet created a climbing community, which grew to include men who would conquer Everest in the 1950s

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads to London's West End as he explores 19th-century engineering used in theatres, and learns about the ambitious building programme that saw Trafalgar Square replace slums. He indulges in some retail therapy at a Piccadilly Circus perfumery patronised by kings, queens and prime ministers, and finds out how diseases were rife in Victorian London on a visit to Soho

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on his final journey in London, exploring Albertopolis in south Kensington and reaching dizzying heights inside a Victorian landmark. At Vauxhall, he learns about the darker side of London's flower market in Bradshaw's day and ends at London Bridge, where two stations are becoming one and a new concourse is being built

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a new journey following his Bradshaw's handbook from the heart of the industrial East Midlands to the island of Lindisfarne off the coast of Northumberland. On the first leg, he gives an old engine a fresh start in the railway hub of Derby, heads to Nottingham to find out about the Victorian origins of a well-known high-street chemist and calls at a historic one-time grocer's shop in Grantham, Lincolnshire

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey from Derby to Lindisfarne, with this leg beginning in Boston, south Lincolnshire, where he explores the connection between the town and its American namesake. At Southwell, Nottinghamshire, he discovers the origins of a favourite Victorian apple before heading into West Yorkshire to visit an imposing institution built in Menston to provide asylum for those with mental health issues. In nearby Wakefield, Michael boards one of Britain's least frequent services, before heading to Hensall, North Yorkshire

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins this leg of his journey in the East Yorkshire town of Hessle, where he learns about the technology that made the Humber Bridge possible. In nearby Hull, Michael hears from local MP Alan Johnson about anti-slavery campaigner William Wilberforce, before travelling to Scarborough Castle, where the founder of the Quaker movement was once imprisoned. His last stop of the day is York, where he learns what made the city a centre for the sweet-making industry

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletcher takes on the role of agony aunt and ends up writing letters home for his fellow inmates - but he soon discovers domestic problems of his own when his daughter brings bad news on visiting day. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Patricia Brake

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The disappearance of an illicit tin of pineapple chunks leaves the inmates horrified that there is a thief in their midst - and for a while, no one is above suspicion. Prison comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Godber is feeling dejected after his romantic fortunes take a turn for the worse, so Fletch tries to revive his friend's flagging spirits. However, his cunning plan has one potential drawback - it involves his own daughter. Comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Guitar Heroes at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive footage of guitarists taken from BBC music shows of the 1970s. Includes electric performances by Carlos Santana, Mark Knopfler, Peter Green and the Edge, and acoustic material by John Martyn, John Renbourn, Bert Jansch and Paco Pena

programme.title: Guitar Heroes at the BBC

programme.desc: Hairdresser Kate Eadon hosts the fourth dinner party in Hastings, East Sussex, with a menu styled on a gourmet pub meal. Adrian Carter gets himself in hot water, Sally-Anne Keller reveals a surprise celebrity crush, and the host recounts her own encounter with a famous funnyman

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beauty spa manager Porchia Lee hosts the third dinner party in Leicester, serving up a French-themed menu of chicken liver pate, pan-fried chicken with tarragon sauce and creme brulee. Unfortunately for her the pudding is so bad her guests are reduced to tears, and double glazing salesman Richard Deeping insults her appearance

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: IT enthusiast Ryan Bruce hosts the fourth dinner party in Leicester, and tries to impress his guests with a Spanish-inspired menu cooked in front of them. Entertainment is provided by Ryan's sister, West End actress Charlie Bruce, who puts the contestants through their paces in a salsa lesson

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Double-glazing salesman Richard Deeping hosts the final dinner party in Leicester, and hopes to make up for his boisterous behaviour by impressing the guests with his culinary prowess. In a bid to land higher scores, he puts on his own brand of entertainment, only to reveal a little too much of himself in the process, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Mojito, a Cuban cafe in Brooklyn, New York. The restaurant was the shared dream of two newlyweds who have separated since its opening, and whose constant public quarrelling is distracting staff and driving customers away

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lynne and her sister Debbie seek the perfect place on the beautiful Costa Teguise in Lanzarote. After Lynne's husband Frankie was diagnosed with a terminal illness, Lynne took early retirement to care for him. A holiday home in the sun has been a lifelong dream for this couple, and with Frankie's illness they don't want to wait any longer. With a budget of £150,000, Ben Hillman lines up five suitable properties for them to choose between

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Recently retired Baksho wants to buy a holiday home in the resort of Gran Alacant on Spain's popular Costa Blanca. With a budget of £100,000 and her sister Sukvinder by her side to help, Jasmine Harman sets out to find Baksho the perfect family friendly property

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets a teacher planning to turn a double-decker bus into a mobile classroom, while a family of car enthusiasts from Coventry is working on a camping trailer. Plus, the Spanish tour continues with a visit to a 100-year-old farmhouse on the coast of Asturias, and the Big Build team design a lightweight, flexible and multi-functional wall system for the campervan living pod

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets a Yorkshire couple who plan to convert a German fire engine into a cabin on wheels and go on a road trip across Europe. George also visits a fly-tipping site being transformed into a nature reserve with a community classroom, while the next stage of the Spanish tour features a cutting edge cantilever house

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer are house-hunting in the East Midlands with two couples at different stages in life. Lloyd and Karina are buying their first home together. They prefer the rural feel of the villages around Northampton but want to be close to Lloyd's parents - while also being near transport links for London. Helen and Richard fell in love late in life. They are desperate to get out of rented accommodation - but as Kirstie soon realises, it's all about the kitchen for former chef Richard

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: DCI Adam Gallop believes a man declared missing in 2015 has been murdered - but has so far been unable to locate his body, and the more time passes, the less chance there is of new witnesses or forensic evidence turning up. The police investigation takes officers from an urgent excavation in the woods where the missing man is believeds to have been shot to a journey across Europe in search of clues

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Sarah Kendall has set the early pace in the series, but fellow contestants Charlotte Ritchie, Jamali Maddix, Lee Mack and Mike Wozniak still have plenty of time to win points and emerge victorious. Here, Charlotte climbs a ladder with some toilet roll, Mike tries out a new catchphrase and Lee terrifies an innocent member of the public. Alex Horne helps coax the contestants as they attempt to complete Greg Davies' tasks

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Matt Lucas welcomes TV presenter Anneka Rice, comedian John Bishop, pop star Nadine Coyle and wheelchair basketball player-turned-presenter Ade Adepitan into the tent. programme.title: Snackmasters
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix challenges two leading chefs to tackle KFC's Zinger Burger and replicate their original top-secret chicken recipe. Tom Aikens from Muse in Belgravia and Alex Bond from Nottingham's Alchemilla have just seven days to perfect their recipes, before cooking them head to head for KFC's big bosses, who will choose a winner. As the chefs take different but equally outlandish approaches in their quests for a perfect replica, comedian Jayde Adams goes behind the scenes of KFC's factories to reveal how the famous products are really made

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Wokingham in Berkshire to meet professional gardener Mark whose barns are brimful of bikes, bric-a-brac and baggage

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are in Windsor, Berkshire, to meet Roy, whose garages are stacked full of bikes. Henry picks the one that doesn't work to take away, while Simon converts some 17th-century wooden beams into a pair of stylish bedside lamps

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Max McMurdo head to Herefordshire to meet George, who has been collecting for more than 70 years. Max finds a crate of vintage motor oil bottles that he thinks could be worth a small fortune

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Hannah, with the help of her friend Helen, is looking for a modern, two-bed apartment with a sea view on the stunning south-west coast of Majorca. But with properties in high demand, can Jasmine Harman find Hannah her dream home for her budget of £250,000?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Postal workers Linda and Andy from Leicestershire are hanging up their mailbags to relocate to the sun-kissed coast of western Cyprus. Jonnie Irwin has five options lined up for their £120,000 budget, but will any have the couple ready to put in an offer?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps a couple from the Wirral who are approaching retirement to find a holiday home in Tenerife's Costa Adeje on a budget of £150,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Maggie and Alistair from Edinburgh dream of buying a holiday home on the southern coast of Gran Canari, famous for its famous white sandy beaches. Jasmine Harman shows Maggie and Alistair five potential properties, but will any of them prove to be their dream holiday home?

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick and Angel as they host a very special wedding for close friends. Fiona puts her B&B plans on hold as she races against time to prepare for her first wedding. With the reception set to be outside she needs to fix 40 windows on the ageing chateau facade

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim and Rebecca battle to get their chateau looking tip top for a tour operator who may potentially want to put it on his books for visitors to the nearby Le Mans motor racing track. The clock is ticking for Fiona as she has 24 hours to prepare for her first ever wedding, and ensure that her 12-acre grounds are picture perfect for the big day. Karen, Paul and their two children need to make their chateau attractive to guests. Their latest plan is to restore a disused hen house, but tensions rise when Paul doesn't meet Karen's standards

programme.title: Scotland's Scenic Railways
programme.desc: A historic section of the East Fife coastal railway is prepared for reopening in a celebration of the golden age of steam. After being single for 24 years, retired dock worker Albert hopes thrill-seeking great-grandmother Carol will be impressed by his yacht, and airline flight dispatcher Ollie thinks he has found the perfect match in cancer nurse Debs when it turns out they are both love knitting and baking

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge tries to bring culture to Springfield by asking architect Frank Gehry (guest starring as himself) to construct a concert hall. However, none of the locals is interested, so Mr Burns decides to turn the disused building into a prison, where Homer soon finds himself confined

programme.desc: A man has to call in eviction specialists to remove tenants from the house he inherited from his grandparents. Music entrepreneur Donovan has not received rent from one of his tenants for seven months, so the case ends up in court

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: A retired secretary has to visit her tenant in prison to retake possession of her buy-to-let flat. A Manchester mother applies to the housing charity Shelter for help because she is convinced the property she is renting is making her children ill
